title: Templated Empathy: A Role-Scoped, Rarity-Calibrated Provenance Audit of Emotional-Support Answers Against the SFT Mixture and Pretraining
one_liner: Decompose small-LLM advice answers into behavioral-move spans and measure what fraction of each move's phrasing is recoverable from assistant turns of the actual SFT mixture (Tulu 3, oasst1) versus pretraining (Dolma/Pile) versus novel composition, contrasting emotional-support vs factual-QA domains — operationalizing the Superficial Alignment Hypothesis at the level of behavioral phrasing.
research_question: For small open-data instruction-tuned LLMs (OLMo-2-1B; Pythia for a scale check), what fraction of an emotional-support / health / relationship answer's BEHAVIORAL structure (empathy openers, validation, hedges, numbered-step scaffolding, "consult a professional" disclaimers) is verbatim-recoverable from ASSISTANT turns of specific SFT conversations (Tulu 3 SFT mixture, oasst1) versus from pretraining web text (Dolma v1.7 via infini-gram) versus is novel composition — and how does that three-way split differ from factual-QA answers and from topical (non-behavioral) content spans within the same answers?
hypotheses: H1 (behavioral vs topical): Within the SAME answer, behavioral-move spans are recoverable from assistant turns of the SFT mixture at a markedly higher rate than topical/content spans of equal length and equal pretraining-frequency band (paired within-answer comparison, so model quirks cancel).

H2 (domain contrast): Emotional-support answers have higher behavioral-span SFT-recoverability and higher behavioral-span coverage than factual-QA answers, concentrated in the empathy/validation/disclaimer move classes; structure (numbered-step) and hedge classes are templated in BOTH domains and will NOT separate them (an explicit, falsifiable null on the move classes reviewers predicted would wash out).

H3 (role-scoping matters): Counting only assistant turns materially lowers SFT match counts versus whole-conversation (blob) matching — i.e. a non-trivial share of naive matches are artifacts of phrases appearing in user turns — establishing role-scoping as necessary for the 'recoverable from SFT' claim to be meaningful. (Pre-verified: 'First,' 7338 blob -> 2238 assistant-only on Tulu shard 0.)

H4 (rarity calibration): Under a naive present/absent test almost all behavioral spans are present in BOTH Dolma and SFT (collapse to 'both'); but when spans are stratified by pretraining-frequency band, distinctively-phrased behavioral moves (low Dolma count, high SFT-assistant count) emerge as an SFT-leaning class, whereas high-frequency moves are corpus-ubiquitous and attributionally uninformative.

H5 (scale/robustness): The behavioral>topical SFT-recoverability gap (H1) is stable across two sampling temperatures and is qualitatively present for a second generator, indicating it is not a single-checkpoint artifact.

method.models: PRIMARY generator: allenai/OLMo-2-0425-1B-Instruct (cached locally, CPU, transformers; generate.py path, temperature 0.7, top_p 0.9, max_new_tokens 256). ROBUSTNESS generators: (a) same model at temperature 0.3 (more deterministic) to test decoding sensitivity; (b) a second instruction-tuned small model run locally on CPU for cross-model breadth — allenai/OLMo-2-1124-7B-Instruct is too large for comfortable CPU, so use Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct or Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct as an OFF-corpus generator (its answers are still traced against OLMo's Dolma+Tulu, which intentionally tests whether templating is corpus-specific or generic). NOTE: pythia-1.4b is base (no chat template) and only pythia-70m is cached; treat any Pythia arm as optional/exploratory, not load-bearing. Attribution corpora are fixed by model: OLMo->Dolma v1.7 (infini-gram index v4_dolma-v1_7_llama) + Tulu 3 + oasst1.
method.datasets: PRETRAINING: Dolma v1.7 via the free infini-gram API (api.infini-gram.io), query_type=count for span attribution and search_docs for sample passages (trace/infinigram.py, trace/ngrams.py). The Pile (v4_piletrain_llama) only if a Pythia arm is run. INSTRUCTION/SFT: allenai/tulu-3-sft-mixture and OpenAssistant/oasst1, read locally from cached parquet via DuckDB (trace/parquet_search.py). Both already cached: Tulu shard 0000.parquet = 361MB (156,558 conversations; messages is a native STRUCT(content,role)[]), oasst1 0000.parquet = 39MB (84,437 message rows; native role column prompter/assistant, plus lang/deleted/review_result). REQUIRED CHANGE: scan ALL 6 Tulu shards (~2GB total download, still local DuckDB, removes the 1/6 low-bias denominator reviewers flagged as inheriting into the headline number); if disk-constrained, keep 1/6 but report SFT recoverability ONLY as the cross-domain RELATIVE contrast, never as an absolute rate. PROMPTS: 50 emotional-support/health/relationship prompts + 50 factual-QA control prompts (closed-form factual questions matched for length and answer structure, e.g. 'how does photosynthesis work', 'what causes tides'), authored and frozen before any generation, released in the repo.
pipeline_steps: 0. PRE-REGISTER the behavioral lexicon and prompt set. Build ~60 behavioral-move phrases (expanded from the proposed ~40 to absorb morphological/contraction variants) grouped into 5 move classes: EMPATHY ('I'm sorry to hear', 'I am sorry to hear', 'that sounds really', 'it sounds like you', 'I understand how'), VALIDATION ('it's completely normal to', 'it is completely normal to', 'there's nothing wrong with', 'many people feel'), HEDGE ('it might help to', 'you may want to consider', 'one option is to', 'you could try'), DISCLAIMER ('consult a', 'speak to a professional', 'speak to a mental health professional', 'I'm not a doctor', 'seek professional help'), STRUCTURE ('Here are a few', 'Here are some', 'First,', 'Second,', 'consider the following'). Each phrase tagged with its variants so coverage is computed over a variant-set, not a single string. Commit the list to git BEFORE generation to defuse the circularity/gameability critique.

1. GENERATE: run OLMo-2-1B on all 100 prompts at temp 0.7 (and a second pass at temp 0.3) via generate.py. ~153s/answer measured -> ~4.3h per pass on CPU, batchable overnight. Save raw answers to out/history.

2. PRETRAINING ATTRIBUTION: run trace/ngrams.attribute(index='v4_dolma-v1_7_llama', answer, max_calls=80) on every answer -> maximal verbatim spans with Dolma counts. ~32s/answer, ~53 min/100 answers. This yields the existing verbatim-span set + per-span Dolma frequency.

3. BEHAVIORAL SPAN TAGGING: for each answer, locate occurrences of every lexicon variant (case-insensitive, word-boundary aware) -> the answer's behavioral spans, each labelled with move class. Separately collect TOPICAL spans = the answer's pretraining-attributed spans (step 2) that do NOT overlap any behavioral span, to serve as the within-answer control population for H1.

4. SFT SEARCH (role-scoped, the key methodological upgrade): extend parquet_search with an assistant_only mode. For Tulu use the native struct: WHERE len(list_filter(messages, m -> m.role='assistant' AND m.content ILIKE $q)) > 0, with the query passed as a DuckDB parameter ($q) so apostrophes are handled natively (raw f-strings break on 'I''m'). For oasst1 use WHERE role='assistant' AND lang='en' AND COALESCE(deleted,false)=false AND text ILIKE $q. Run BOTH whole-conversation (blob, existing behavior) and assistant-only counts so H3's gap is quantified. Each behavioral variant and each topical span is queried -> total_matches per dataset + up to 3 full example conversations.

5. RARITY-CALIBRATED THREE-WAY TAG: for each span (behavioral and topical) record (Dolma_count, SFT_assistant_matches). Assign DOLMA-FREQUENCY BANDS by quantile (e.g. <100, 100-10k, >10k). Define the three-way label not as raw present/absent (which collapses to 'both', per H4) but as: SFT-LEANING (SFT_assistant>0 AND Dolma_count in low band), COMMON (present in both AND Dolma high band), NOVEL (Dolma_count=0 AND SFT_assistant=0). Report the full joint distribution, not just a single split.

6. PRECISION VALIDATION: manually code a stratified random sample of 100 SFT 'hits' (assistant-only matches) — label each as TRUE behavioral move in an assistant answer vs FALSE (code block / quoted user text / unrelated context). Report precision per move class. This converts the correlational substring signal into a calibrated estimate and directly answers the 'substring != provenance' critique with a number.

7. METRICS + STATS: compute per-class behavioral-span coverage, SFT-assistant recoverability rate, and the within-answer behavioral-vs-topical paired difference; test H1 with a paired test (Wilcoxon signed-rank across answers), H2/H5 with Mann-Whitney across domains and a temperature x domain comparison; bootstrap 95% CIs over prompts.
8. WORKED EXAMPLE + REPORT: render one full annotated relationship-advice answer (see expected_findings) using the existing render_report path extended with the move-class tags.

experiments.name: E1 — Behavioral vs topical SFT-recoverability, within answer (tests H1)
experiments.setup: For all 50 support + 50 factual answers (temp 0.7), partition each answer's spans into behavioral (lexicon) and topical (pretraining-attributed, non-behavioral). For each span compute assistant-only SFT match count in Tulu+oasst1. Pair within answer: behavioral-span SFT-recoverable fraction vs topical-span SFT-recoverable fraction. Wilcoxon signed-rank over the 100 answers; control by also matching on Dolma-frequency band so the contrast is not just 'behavioral phrases are shorter/commoner'.
experiments.expected_result: Behavioral spans recoverable from SFT-assistant turns at a substantially higher rate than length-/frequency-matched topical spans (expected gap on the order of tens of percentage points; significant p<0.01). Topical spans (e.g. 'rapid eye movement sleep', a specific relationship detail) rarely appear in SFT-assistant turns; behavioral moves frequently do. This is the core positive result and it is robust to the lexicon-circularity critique because the comparison is WITHIN the model's own emitted spans.

experiments.name: E2 — Domain contrast by move class (tests H2, including the predicted null)
experiments.setup: Compare support vs factual answers on (a) overall behavioral-span coverage and (b) per-move-class SFT-assistant recoverability and frequency-normalized match rate. Mann-Whitney per class with Holm correction.
experiments.expected_result: EMPATHY, VALIDATION, DISCLAIMER classes are far more frequent and more SFT-recoverable in support answers (large, significant separation). STRUCTURE (numbered steps) and HEDGE do NOT separate the domains (pre-registered null confirmed) — both domains scaffold with 'First,/Second,' and 'you may want to'. Reporting this null is a feature: it shows the instrument discriminates where theory predicts and not where it doesn't, defusing the 'water is wet / it'll all wash out' reviewer worry by predicting the wash-out a priori for two specific classes.

experiments.name: E3 — Role-scoping ablation (tests H3, quantifies a method artifact)
experiments.setup: For every behavioral and topical query, compute whole-conversation (blob, current tool behavior) vs assistant-only match counts on Tulu and oasst1. Report the per-class inflation factor blob/assistant and the share of blob matches attributable to user turns.
experiments.expected_result: Large, class-dependent inflation for generic phrases (pre-measured: 'First,' blob=7338 -> assistant=2238, i.e. 70% of blob hits are user turns; 'consult a' 195->162; 'I'm sorry to hear' 74->67). Demonstrates that the naive tool over-counts SFT 'provenance' and that role-scoping is required for a defensible claim. Directly answers the top reviewer risk with a measured table.

experiments.name: E4 — Rarity calibration / three-way collapse (tests H4)
experiments.setup: Tag every behavioral span with (Dolma_count, SFT_assistant_matches). Show the joint distribution and the fraction landing in 'both' under naive present/absent vs under frequency-banding.
experiments.expected_result: Under naive present/absent, ~all behavioral spans are 'present in both' (e.g. 'you may want to consider'=1.6M Dolma hits AND present in SFT), confirming the collapse reviewers predicted. After banding, a minority but meaningful set of DISTINCTIVE behavioral moves (low Dolma band, present in SFT-assistant) emerge as SFT-leaning — this subset is the honest, attributionally-informative core of the headline claim. Reported as a calibration curve, not a single fragile percentage.

experiments.name: E5 — Precision audit of SFT hits (validates the central claim)
experiments.setup: Manually code 100 stratified assistant-only Tulu/oasst1 hits as true behavioral move vs false context (code/quote/unrelated). Compute precision per move class; recompute E1/E2 headline numbers weighted by per-class precision.
experiments.expected_result: High precision for empathy/validation/disclaimer (these phrases are near-always genuine assistant moves), lower for STRUCTURE ('First,' sometimes inside enumerations of unrelated content). Precision-weighting shifts magnitudes modestly but not the direction of E1/E2, converting a correlational signal into a validated estimate.

experiments.name: E6 — Robustness: temperature and second generator (tests H5)
experiments.setup: Repeat E1's behavioral>topical gap at temp 0.3 and for a second small instruction model (Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct or Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct) traced against the SAME OLMo corpora. Compare effect sign and magnitude.
experiments.expected_result: The behavioral>topical gap persists at both temperatures (decoding-insensitive). For the off-corpus second model the gap is present but attenuated/qualitatively different, showing some behavioral phrasing is generic-web-shared while a residual is OLMo-SFT-specific — a clean, honest cross-model caveat that scopes the SAH operationalization rather than over-claiming generality from one model.

metrics: Behavioral-span coverage = (number of answer word-tokens inside any behavioral-lexicon span) / (total answer word-tokens). Reported overall and per move class.

SFT-assistant recoverability rate of a span = 1 if the span (any variant) has >=1 match in ASSISTANT turns of Tulu+oasst1 (role-scoped, parameterized ILIKE), else 0. Aggregated as the fraction of behavioral spans recoverable, and separately for topical spans (the within-answer control).

Behavioral-minus-topical recoverability gap = per-answer difference in SFT-assistant recoverability between its behavioral and frequency-band-matched topical spans (the H1 effect size); reported with Wilcoxon p and bootstrap 95% CI.

Three-way rarity-calibrated label distribution per span: %SFT-leaning (SFT-assistant>0 AND Dolma low band) / %common (both, Dolma high band) / %novel (Dolma=0 AND SFT=0). Reported as a distribution, not a single number.
Role-scoping inflation factor = blob_matches / assistant_only_matches, per move class (the H3 artifact size).
Pretraining verbatim-span coverage (existing tool metric) = sum of word counts of Dolma-matched spans / total answer words; reported per domain as a cross-layer comparator.
Cross-layer ratio = (behavioral spans SFT-recoverable) / (behavioral spans Dolma-present), per move class — high=SFT-leaning behavior, low=pretraining-ubiquitous.
Frequency-normalized SFT match rate = SFT-assistant matches per 100k assistant turns scanned, so high-frequency phrases ('First,') are normalized rather than dominating raw totals.
Precision of SFT hits (manual) = fraction of audited assistant-only matches that are genuine behavioral moves, per move class.
Domain separation per move class = effect size (rank-biserial r) + Holm-corrected p for support vs factual.
Temperature/model robustness = sign-agreement and magnitude ratio of the E1 gap across temp 0.3/0.7 and across the two generators.
baselines: Topical-span baseline (PRIMARY, within-answer): the same answer's pretraining-attributed non-behavioral spans, frequency-band-matched. This is the load-bearing control that makes the behavioral claim robust to model quirks and lexicon choice — it asks 'are BEHAVIORAL spans more SFT-recoverable than this model's other emitted spans?'.
Factual-QA domain baseline: the 50 control answers, giving the cross-domain contrast that survives even under 1/6 Tulu sampling (sampling bias is identical across arms).
Blob (whole-conversation) baseline vs role-scoped: the existing tool's to_json(messages) search is the naive baseline against which role-scoping is the improvement (E3).
Naive present/absent three-way tag vs rarity-banded tag: the uncalibrated split is the baseline that collapses to 'both' (E4), motivating the calibrated metric.

Random-phrase baseline: a set of ~60 length-matched, non-behavioral 4-6 word phrases sampled from the support answers' own topical content, to bound the false-positive SFT-match rate of generic English n-grams.

Lexicon-robustness baseline: recompute all headline numbers under a held-out alternative lexicon (variant phrasings authored by a second person from the same move-class definitions) to report sensitivity of coverage to the exact phrase list.
threats_to_validity: Substring co-occurrence != causal provenance. A behavioral phrase in an SFT-assistant turn AND in Dolma cannot prove the model learned the move from SFT. MITIGATION: frame results as 'verbatim-recoverable from assistant turns of the SFT mixture' (a membership/overlap claim, explicitly NOT a causal one); lead with the within-answer behavioral-vs-topical CONTRAST and the cross-domain CONTRAST (both differential, less sensitive to the shared-corpus problem) rather than absolute rates; report the rarity-calibrated SFT-leaning subset as the only attributionally-suggestive slice.

Lexicon is the instrument and is hand-built (gameability/circularity). MITIGATION: pre-register and git-commit the ~60-phrase variant-aware lexicon before generation; report a held-out alternative-lexicon robustness run; anchor the headline on the within-answer behavioral-vs-topical comparison, which holds whatever phrases the model emits, not just listed ones.

ILIKE is context-blind (user turns, code blocks). MITIGATION: role-scoped assistant-only matching via native struct/role column (measured to remove up to ~70% of spurious blob matches for generic phrases) + a manual precision audit (E5) reporting per-class true-positive rates.

Punctuation/JSON-escaping distortion on contractions. MITIGATION: parameterized DuckDB queries on the native messages STRUCT and oasst1 text column (apostrophes handled natively) instead of f-string ILIKE on to_json blobs; verified working.

Pretraining counts dominate; 3-way split collapses to 'both'. MITIGATION: Dolma-frequency banding + report the joint (Dolma_count, SFT_match) distribution and a calibration curve rather than a single fragile fraction (E4).

Tulu 1/6 sampling biases SFT denominators low. MITIGATION: scan all 6 shards (~2GB, still local) for the main result; if kept at 1/6, report SFT recoverability ONLY as the cross-domain/within-answer relative contrast (the bias is common-mode across arms) and never as an extrapolable absolute rate.

Single weak 1B generator may be unrepresentative; SAH generalization over-claimed. MITIGATION: two temperatures + a second small model; scope claims to 'small open instruction-tuned models' and present SAH as an OPERATIONALIZATION/measurement contribution, not a settled verdict on deployed assistants.

Predetermined/low-information result ('alignment teaches scaffolding'). MITIGATION: the informative content is the DIFFERENTIAL structure — which move classes separate domains and which do NOT (pre-registered nulls on STRUCTURE/HEDGE), the role-scoping artifact size, and the rarity-calibrated SFT-leaning subset — none of which are obvious a priori.

Sensitive-domain safety scrutiny of 1B outputs. MITIGATION: study object is PROVENANCE of phrasing, not advice quality; add a brief safety/appropriateness note on sampled outputs and avoid presenting any generated advice as guidance; restrict prompts to non-crisis everyday support topics.

Paraphrastic provenance missed by verbatim/substring methods. MITIGATION: acknowledge as a scope boundary; optionally add a cheap embedding extension (intfloat/multilingual-e5-small, already cached) computing cosine similarity of answer behavioral spans to nearest SFT-assistant turns to bound how much paraphrastic SFT overlap the verbatim method misses.
deliverables: A frozen, git-committed behavioral-move lexicon (~60 phrases, variant-aware, 5 classes) + the 100-prompt set (50 support, 50 factual), released for reproducibility.

A small code patch to trace/parquet_search.py adding role-scoped (assistant-only) parameterized search for Tulu (native STRUCT list_filter) and oasst1 (role+lang+deleted filter), plus an option to scan all 6 Tulu shards — a concrete, upstreamable improvement to the tracer.

A new analysis script (per the tool spec, the coverage roll-up is post-hoc, not yet in pipeline.py) computing per-class behavioral-span coverage, the rarity-calibrated three-way distribution, and the within-answer paired statistics, writing a per-answer JSONL + summary tables.

Results tables/figures: (T1) role-scoping inflation by move class; (T2) behavioral-vs-topical recoverability paired result; (T3) domain x move-class separation with the pre-registered nulls; (F1) Dolma-frequency vs SFT-match calibration scatter; (T4) precision audit by class.
A fully worked, annotated relationship-advice example (answer with color-coded behavioral spans, each tagged Dolma-count / SFT-assistant-match / class) as the paper's figure 1.

An 8-page workshop paper + appendix (lexicon, prompts, precision-audit codebook, all numbers with bootstrap CIs), plus the exported trace JSON/MD records from the existing history/export pipeline as a reproducibility bundle.
An optional embedding-paraphrase appendix (e5-small, cached) bounding missed paraphrastic SFT overlap.
target_venue: Primary: a 2026 NLP-workshop on memorization/attribution/data provenance — e.g. an ACL/EMNLP/NeurICS-adjacent workshop in the lineage of the L2M2 (Large Language Model Memorization) workshop at ACL 2025, or BlackboxNLP (EMNLP) for the interpretability/behavioral-provenance framing. Strong secondary fits: a Data-Centric ML / data attribution workshop (DMLR / ATTRIB-style) at NeurIPS or ICLR, or the TrustNLP / safety-in-NLP workshop given the sensitive-advice angle. Stretch: EMNLP/ACL Findings if the role-scoping method, the rarity calibration, and the precision-validated within-answer contrast are developed into a fuller study with the all-6-shard Tulu scan and a second corpus (Pythia/Pile) arm.
effort: 3-4 weeks for one researcher, comfortably within CPU-only/free-API constraints. Compute envelope (all measured on this workstation): generation 2 temperature passes x 100 answers x ~153s ~= 8.6h (overnight, batchable); pretraining attribution 100 answers x ~32s (80-call infini-gram cap) ~= 53 min/pass; role-scoped SFT lexicon+topical sweep ~6-20s/query cached locally, one-time few hours total; manual precision audit ~half a day. Extra resources beyond the workstation: ~2GB additional disk + one-time HF-authenticated download if scanning all 6 Tulu shards (the only thing beyond the already-cached 361MB+39MB); HF read token already required (and per the standing memory note, the previously-exposed token must be rotated first); no GPU, no paid APIs, no closed-model internals. Second-generator arm adds a one-time model download (Qwen2.5-0.5B ~1GB / Llama-3.2-1B ~2.5GB) and another ~4h CPU pass. Pythia/Pile arm is optional and would add The-Pile infini-gram calls only (pythia-1.4b not currently cached).
expected_findings: Headline: behavioral-move spans in small-LLM advice answers are recoverable from ASSISTANT turns of the SFT mixture at substantially higher rates than the model's own length-/frequency-matched topical spans (within-answer effect, robust to lexicon and model quirks), and emotional-support answers are more behaviorally templated than factual-QA answers SPECIFICALLY in empathy/validation/disclaimer classes — while numbered-step and hedge scaffolding are equally template-y in both (a pre-registered null that the instrument correctly recovers). Two methodological results give the paper teeth: (1) role-scoping is necessary — naive whole-conversation matching inflates 'SFT provenance' by up to ~70% for generic phrases (measured: 'First,' 7338->2238); (2) the naive pretrain/SFT/novel split collapses to 'present in both' (measured: 'you may want to consider'=1.6M Dolma hits), and only after Dolma-frequency banding does a distinctive SFT-leaning subset of behavioral moves emerge. WORKED EXAMPLE (relationship domain): for the prompt 'I just broke up with my girlfriend, what should I do', OLMo-2-1B opens 'It sounds like you're going through a difficult time' (EMPATHY; high in both Dolma and Tulu-assistant -> COMMON), validates 'it's completely normal to feel this way' (VALIDATION; Dolma=1,480 / Tulu-assistant=7 -> SFT-LEANING after banding), scaffolds with 'Here are a few things you can do: First,...' (STRUCTURE; ubiquitous, does not discriminate domain), and closes 'consider speaking to a professional' (DISCLAIMER; Tulu-assistant present, low Dolma band -> SFT-LEANING), while the breakup-specific topical content has near-zero SFT-assistant matches — visually demonstrating that the answer's BEHAVIORAL skeleton is SFT-shaped and its topical flesh is not. Net contribution: the first role-scoped, rarity-calibrated, precision-validated operationalization of behavioral-phrasing provenance against an ACTUAL SFT mixture in a socially high-stakes domain — honestly scoped as overlap/membership, not causation, and as small-open-model, not deployed-assistant.

title: The Disclaimer Reflex: Source-Document Provenance of Safety Boilerplate in LLM Health and Mental-Health Advice
one_liner: We trace the exact disclaimer and crisis-referral phrasings an open-data LLM (OLMo-2-1B) emits on health/mental-health prompts back to their named Dolma source documents (reddit/cc_en_head/falcon/c4) and to specific full Tulu-3 and oasst1 SFT conversations, quantifying how much of the "safety reflex" is verbatim corpus boilerplate vs. learned-but-not-copied behavior — framed strictly as verbatim overlap/co-occurrence, not causal extraction.
research_question: Where does the safety/disclaimer reflex in LLM health and mental-health advice come from at the level of surface phrasing: which disclaimer and crisis-referral phrases recur across answers, how rare/common are they in the actual pretraining corpus, which corpus source domains (reddit, cc_en_head/middle/tail, falcon, c4, books) supply them, and which specific full SFT conversations in Tulu-3 and oasst1 contain the same phrasing? Operationally: what fraction of the disclaimer move is verbatim-recoverable from pretraining vs. from instruction data vs. neither (novel composition)?
hypotheses: H1 (templating): The set of distinct disclaimer/referral phrasings OLMo-2-1B emits across ~40 prompts is small and highly repeated — a handful of recurring templates (e.g. 'consult a healthcare professional', 'I am not a doctor', 'please seek professional help') account for the large majority of disclaimer tokens (high span-repetition, low type/token ratio of disclaimer phrasings).

H2 (pretraining ubiquity, rarity-graded): Generic disclaimers ('consult a professional', 'talk to someone you trust') have very high Dolma counts (10^4-10^5), while crisis-specific phrasings ('call or text 988', 'the Suicide and Crisis Lifeline') are rarer (10^2-10^3 or lower), so verbatim provenance strength is inversely graded by specificity — the rare, safety-critical phrasings are the ones with identifiable, concentrated source provenance.

H3 (source-domain signature): Disclaimer phrasings are disproportionately sourced from a distinctive subset of Dolma domains relative to the answer's non-disclaimer prose — specifically cc_en_head health/advice sites (e.g. marriage.com, healthline-like pages) and reddit advice subreddits — rather than uniformly across the corpus, giving the disclaimer move a measurable source-domain signature.

H4 (SFT inventory exists and is enumerable only with full Tulu-3): The exact disclaimer phrasings appear verbatim in a countable, inspectable set of full Tulu-3/oasst1 conversations, but the count and template inventory are materially undercounted at the default 1/6 Tulu-3 sample (>=~6x under), so an honest 'small identifiable set' claim requires the un-capped full-Tulu-3 scan.

H5 (two-layer decomposition): For sensitive-advice answers, a non-trivial fraction of disclaimer tokens are simultaneously present verbatim in BOTH pretraining and SFT, more so than for the answer's topical/body prose, i.e. the safety move is the most reproduced (least novel) part of the answer.

H6 (weak-model caveat / falsifiable DV): OLMo-2-1B emits clean, well-formed disclaimer boilerplate on a substantial share of health prompts but emits crisis-referral phrasing (988/hotline) rarely or never; if the pilot shows the DV is too sparse, the crisis sub-arm is descriptively reported as a near-null and the study pivots to the general-disclaimer arm.

method.models: Generation: allenai/OLMo-2-0425-1B-Instruct run locally on CPU via the tracer's trace.generate.generate('olmo2', question) (transformers, chat template, temperature=0.7, top_p=0.9, max_new_tokens=256). Primary model. Optional scale arm: EleutherAI/pythia-1.4b base (trace index v4_piletrain_llama) — base model, no chat template, expected to emit far fewer disclaimers; used only as a contrast, not a main result. Optional proxy arm: one frontier closed model (Claude via Anthropic API) generated answers, traced ONLY against Dolma and flagged loudly as proxy (is_proxy), included to show a strong-model disclaimer profile for contrast, never claimed as real provenance. Attribution corpora are queried, not run: Dolma v1.7 via infini-gram index v4_dolma-v1_7_llama (free public API).
method.datasets: Pretraining corpus (queried, not downloaded): Dolma v1.7 via infini-gram (config.PRETRAIN_INDEXES['olmo2'] = 'v4_dolma-v1_7_llama'); The Pile (v4_piletrain_llama) only for the optional Pythia arm. Instruction corpora (local parquet via DuckDB, already cached under out/cache/): allenai/tulu-3-sft-mixture (~939k convos; default code caps to 1/6 shards via parquet_search._SHARD_CAP — MUST be set to None for the honest full scan, a one-time ~2.1GB download) and OpenAssistant/oasst1 (full, ~39MB, already cached). Prompt set: ~40 curated health/mental-health questions in 4 strata (~10 each): (A) physical-health self-care ('how do I stop snoring', 'how do I lower my blood pressure'), (B) mental-health/emotional-support ('I feel anxious all the time', 'I think I'm depressed'), (C) relationship/advice running-domain ('my girlfriend broke up with me', 'how do I get over a breakup'), (D) crisis-adjacent, handled strictly as research artifacts, never deployed ('I feel hopeless and don't want to be here', 'I can't stop thinking about hurting myself'). Stratum D is the most safety-relevant but expected to be the thinnest from a 1B model.
pipeline_steps: STEP 0 — PILOT FIRST (gating, ~1-2 hrs): run trace.generate.generate('olmo2', q) on 5-8 prompts spanning all 4 strata. Manually verify OLMo-2-1B actually emits well-formed disclaimer phrasing and check whether ANY crisis-referral phrasing (988, hotline, 'reach out to a crisis line') appears in stratum D. Decision gate: if disclaimers are present in >=~50% of A-C answers, proceed; if crisis phrasing is absent in D, pre-register the crisis arm as descriptive/near-null rather than a headline. This directly de-risks the dependent-variable risk before committing compute.

STEP 1 — Generation (~2-4 unattended CPU hours): generate one OLMo-2-1B answer per prompt (40 answers), greedy/temp-0.7 as configured; also generate 3 answers per prompt with different seeds for a subset to measure phrasing stability (does the SAME disclaimer template recur across samples — evidence for 'reflex'). Save all via the existing history/store mechanism.

STEP 2 — Disclaimer-phrase extraction (build the dependent variable): from the generated answers, extract candidate disclaimer/referral spans using a curated regex/lexicon of ~25-40 canonical safety phrasings (e.g. 'I am not a doctor', 'I'm not a (medical|mental health) professional', 'consult (a|your) (doctor|healthcare professional|physician)', 'seek professional help', 'speak (to|with) a (therapist|professional)', 'talk to someone you trust', 'call or text 988', 'Suicide and Crisis Lifeline', 'reach out to a crisis (line|hotline)', 'if you are in (crisis|danger)'). This lexicon is the unit of analysis; report it in full for reproducibility. Normalize each matched phrase to its surface form as emitted.

STEP 3 — Pretraining provenance per phrase (infini-gram): for each distinct emitted disclaimer phrase P, call trace.infinigram.count('v4_dolma-v1_7_llama', P) for the exact-phrase Dolma count (rarity), then trace.infinigram.passages_for('v4_dolma-v1_7_llama', P, maxnum=10) to retrieve up to 10 actual source documents with metadata. This bypasses the greedy longest-match (which fragments fixed phrases at the first OOV word) and queries the FULL disclaimer phrase directly — exactly the calls confirmed in trace/infinigram.py. Respect the 80-call/trace budget by batching at the phrase level (≈25-40 unique phrases total across the whole study, not per answer), well within free-API limits.

STEP 4 — Source-domain bucketing (the headline metric; needs a small new helper, ~30 lines): write bucket_source(src) that reads src['path'] (first segment) and src['metadata'] (source/subreddit/source_domain) and maps each returned passage to a domain bucket {reddit, cc_en_head, cc_en_middle, cc_en_tail, falcon, c4, books, wiki, stack, other/unknown}. This extends the existing _src_tag (pipeline.py:60, which only returns raw url/path). For reddit, also capture metadata.metadata.subreddit (e.g. 'relationships', 'depression') for sub-source granularity. Tabulate the source-domain distribution per phrase and pooled across all disclaimer phrases; add an explicit 'unknown' bucket for docs lacking a clean tag.

STEP 5 — Instruction provenance per phrase (parquet/DuckDB): un-cap Tulu-3 by setting parquet_search._SHARD_CAP['allenai/tulu-3-sft-mixture'] = None (one-time ~2.1GB download), then for each distinct disclaimer phrase P call trace.parquet_search.search('allenai/tulu-3-sft-mixture', P, limit=N) and trace.parquet_search.search('OpenAssistant/oasst1', P, limit=N). This uses the raw ILIKE '%P%' substring path directly (confirmed in parquet_search.py), bypassing hf_datasets.search_instruction's rarest-term picker which degrades to typos like 'withotu'. Record total_matches (now full-dataset for Tulu-3), the sampled flag (should be false after un-capping), and retrieve the FULL multi-turn conversations for inspection.

STEP 6 — SFT template clustering (the novel arm): for each disclaimer phrase, manually/semi-automatically cluster the retrieved SFT conversations by (a) is the disclaimer topic-prompted (user explicitly asks something medical/crisis) vs. reflexive (model adds it unprompted to an unrelated topic), and (b) by conversation template family (e.g. 'persona safety system prompt', 'medical Q&A with mandatory disclaimer', 'roleplay refusal'). Report the count of distinct template families per phrase. Optional cheap embedding pass (a ~10MB MiniLM model on CPU) to cluster >50 retrieved convos when manual is infeasible — listed as an extension, not required.

STEP 7 — Two-layer decomposition & metrics: for each answer compute disclaimer-token coverage by pretraining, by SFT, by both, and by neither; compare against the same decomposition for the answer's non-disclaimer body text (using the existing trace.ngrams.attribute longest-match over the body as the body baseline). Aggregate across strata.

STEP 8 — Reporting: per-phrase table (phrase, words, Dolma count, top-3 source domains, top reddit subreddits, full-Tulu-3 match count, oasst1 match count, # distinct SFT template families) plus the pooled source-domain distribution and the cross-layer decomposition figure. Export via the existing JSON/MD/CSV history exporters.

experiments.name: E0 — Dependent-variable pilot (gating)
experiments.setup: Generate OLMo-2-1B answers for 5-8 prompts across strata A-D; manually score presence/quality of disclaimer phrasing and any crisis-referral language. No tracing yet.
experiments.expected_result: Generic disclaimers ('consult a professional', 'I'm not a doctor') appear in roughly half-plus of A-C answers; crisis-referral phrasing (988/hotline) appears rarely or not at all in D. Confirms the general-disclaimer arm is viable and pre-registers the crisis arm as likely near-null — converting the biggest risk into a stated, falsifiable finding rather than a surprise failure.

experiments.name: E1 — Disclaimer rarity spectrum in Dolma
experiments.setup: For every distinct emitted disclaimer phrase, infinigram.count over Dolma; plot the count distribution and correlate count with phrase specificity (generic vs crisis-specific), using the lexicon stratification.
experiments.expected_result: Counts span >=3 orders of magnitude. Generic phrasings land 10^4-10^5 (e.g. confirmed live: 'I am not a doctor'≈102,609; 'consult a healthcare professional'≈25,171), specific ones lower (e.g. confirmed live: 'consult with a mental health professional'≈2,604; 'please seek professional help'≈8,775; 'If you are in crisis'≈5,277). Supports H2: rarity grades inversely with safety-specificity, so the safety-critical phrasings are the ones with tractable, concentrated provenance — and pre-empts the 'common phrases are common' null by making rarity the explicit axis.

experiments.name: E2 — Source-domain signature of the disclaimer move
experiments.setup: For each disclaimer phrase, passages_for(maxnum=10) over Dolma, bucket via bucket_source(); compare the pooled disclaimer source-domain distribution against the source-domain distribution of the answers' non-disclaimer body spans (from ngrams.attribute).
experiments.expected_result: Disclaimer phrasings concentrate in cc_en_head health/advice domains and reddit advice subreddits (the prior relationship trace already shows reddit r/relationships, cc_en_head marriage.com, and falcon as live sources for advice phrasing), with a distinguishable signature vs. body prose. Supports H3; gives the paper its memorable 'where the disclaimer literally comes from on the web' visualization.

experiments.name: E3 — Full-Tulu-3 vs 1/6-sample SFT counts (methodological)
experiments.setup: For each disclaimer phrase, run parquet_search.search on Tulu-3 BOTH at default cap=1 and at cap=None, plus oasst1 full; record total_matches under each.
experiments.expected_result: Full-scan Tulu-3 counts are materially higher (roughly multiplicative in shards scanned) and some phrases that show 0 hits at 1/6 are non-zero at full scan. Quantifies the sampling bias the reviewers flagged and justifies the un-capped scan as a precondition for any 'identifiable set of SFT templates' claim (H4). Doubles as a reusable cautionary result about the tool's default.

experiments.name: E4 — SFT template inventory & reflexive-vs-prompted split
experiments.setup: On full-Tulu-3 + oasst1 hits per disclaimer phrase, cluster retrieved full conversations into template families and label topic-prompted vs reflexive.
experiments.expected_result: A small number (single digits to low tens) of distinct template families dominate per phrase, and a measurable share are reflexive (disclaimer appended to non-medical prompts). Supports H1/H4; if instead matches are huge and topically scattered, that itself is the finding (ubiquity, not a clean template set) — reported honestly either way.

experiments.name: E5 — Two-layer novelty decomposition (sensitive vs body)
experiments.setup: Per answer, compute disclaimer-token coverage by {pretraining-only, SFT-only, both, neither} and contrast with the same decomposition for non-disclaimer body text via ngrams.attribute.
experiments.expected_result: Disclaimer spans show higher verbatim coverage (lower novelty) and higher both-layer overlap than body prose, i.e. the safety move is the least novel, most reproduced part of the answer. Supports H5 and grounds the 'surface reproduction' framing as a measured overlap quantity, NOT a causal extraction claim.

experiments.name: E6 (optional) — Scale/model contrast
experiments.setup: Repeat E1-E2 with pythia-1.4b (Pile index) and with one Claude proxy-traced arm (Dolma, flagged proxy). Descriptive only.
experiments.expected_result: Pythia base emits far fewer/looser disclaimers (weaker reflex); Claude emits crisp, frequent disclaimers including crisis referrals. Shows the reflex strengthens with post-training/scale, contextualizing the OLMo-2-1B result and answering the 'n=1 weak model' reviewer concern without overclaiming.

metrics: Disclaimer emission rate: fraction of the ~40 answers (per stratum) containing >=1 lexicon-matched disclaimer/referral phrase. Crisis-referral emission rate: same restricted to crisis-specific phrasings.

Disclaimer phrasing repetition / template concentration: number of DISTINCT emitted disclaimer phrasings vs total disclaimer occurrences (type/token ratio); Gini/top-k share of the most-repeated phrasings. Low ratio = templated reflex (H1).
Per-phrase Dolma rarity: exact-phrase infinigram count (log scale), and rarity-vs-specificity correlation.

Pretraining source-domain distribution (HEADLINE): per-phrase and pooled percentage of returned passages per bucket {reddit, cc_en_head/middle/tail, falcon, c4, books, wiki, stack, other, unknown}; reddit sub-source = subreddit histogram. Includes an explicit 'unknown' bucket fraction as a data-quality readout.
Source concentration: top-3 source domains as % of all disclaimer passages; compared to top-3 for body-text passages (signature contrast).
SFT verbatim presence: full-Tulu-3 total_matches and oasst1 total_matches per disclaimer phrase (with sampled=false asserted); plus the 1/6-vs-full ratio as a bias metric.
SFT template inventory: count of distinct template families per phrase, and reflexive-vs-prompted proportion among retrieved conversations.

Cross-layer disclaimer decomposition: per-answer disclaimer-token coverage by {pretraining-only, SFT-only, both, neither}, aggregated by stratum; and the same for non-disclaimer body text as the within-answer baseline.
Phrasing stability across seeds: for the multi-seed subset, fraction of resamples that reproduce the same disclaimer template (reflex robustness).
Verbatim-overlap ratio for disclaimer spans vs body spans (existing tool metric: matched-span word coverage / total words), reported separately for the two regions.
baselines: Within-answer body-text baseline: same provenance pipeline (ngrams.attribute longest-match + source bucketing) applied to the NON-disclaimer prose of each answer. This is the key control — it isolates whether the disclaimer move is more templated/more reproduced/source-concentrated than ordinary advice prose, defending against the 'everything common is common' null.

Topic-matched non-safety phrase baseline: a lexicon of equally-frequent NON-safety stock phrases the model emits in the same answers (e.g. 'it's important to remember', 'here are a few things you can try') run through the identical count + source-bucket pipeline, to show source-domain signatures are specific to disclaimers, not to any boilerplate.

Frequency-matched random n-gram baseline: random 4-8 word spans sampled from answer body, matched on Dolma count band, to calibrate the expected source-domain distribution at a given rarity (controls H3 against a pure-frequency confound).
Sampling baseline (methodological): Tulu-3 at default 1/6 cap vs un-capped, as the explicit before/after for E3.
Model/scale baselines (optional): Pythia-1.4b (Pile) and a Claude proxy arm as upper/lower disclaimer-strength references.
threats_to_validity: CAUSALITY OVERREACH (primary): substring/verbatim presence in Dolma or Tulu-3 shows the phrasing CO-OCCURS in the data, not that OLMo-2-1B causally lifted it from those documents. Mitigation: frame every claim as verbatim overlap / provenance candidate / co-occurrence; explicitly disclaim causality (matching OLMoTrace's stance); position lexical provenance as a candidate generator for, not a substitute for, influence-function evidence; never say 'extracted from'.

WEAK DEPENDENT VARIABLE: a 1B model's disclaimer reflex may be sparse, malformed, or (for crisis phrasing) absent, hollowing the safety angle. Mitigation: E0 pilot gates the whole study; crisis arm pre-registered as possibly near-null and reported descriptively; optional Claude/Pythia arms bracket the result so conclusions don't rest on one weak model.

SFT UNDERCOUNT FROM SAMPLING: default 1/6 Tulu-3 cap biases total_matches low and can yield false 0s, undermining 'small identifiable set of templates'. Mitigation: un-cap to None for all SFT counts (E3 quantifies the bias); never report 1/6-sample counts as the inventory.

GENERIC-PHRASE NOISE / NULL RISK: ultra-common disclaimers return 10^4-10^5 Dolma hits and numerous scattered SFT matches, so 'common phrases are common' could be the only finding. Mitigation: rarity is an explicit axis (E1), crisis-specific (rarer) phrasings are the analytic focus, and the body-text + frequency-matched baselines convert ubiquity into a comparative signal.

SOURCE-METADATA GAPS: some Dolma docs lack a clean path/source tag, inflating the 'unknown' bucket and biasing the source-domain distribution. Mitigation: report the unknown fraction transparently; bucket from both path and nested metadata.source/source_domain; treat domains only at the coarse bucket level where tags are reliable.

NOVELTY IS INCREMENTAL, NOT NEW-THESIS: 'safety boilerplate is templated' is close to known (shallow-alignment, refusal-direction). Mitigation: the contribution is the provenance INSTANTIATION — naming specific Dolma source documents/domains and specific full SFT conversations for the disclaimer move in an unstudied high-stakes domain — explicitly positioned as a measurement/transparency contribution, not a new mechanism claim.

GREEDY-MATCH FRAGMENTATION: the tool's longest-PREFIX matcher would split a fixed disclaimer at the first OOV word; the study avoids this by querying full curated disclaimer phrases directly via infinigram.count, so the matcher quirk does not affect the disclaimer arm (and is noted as a limitation only for the body-text baseline).

ETHICS / SENSITIVE CONTENT: crisis-adjacent prompts and self-harm content require care. Mitigation: prompts handled strictly as research artifacts, never deployed; no real user data; report aggregate phrasings, not generated harmful content verbatim beyond the short disclaimer spans; include an ethics/broader-impacts statement; flag at submission that the work characterizes (does not build) advice systems.

API VARIANCE: infini-gram free-API latency/quota on a multi-phrase batch. Mitigation: ≈25-40 unique phrase queries total (phrase-level, not per-answer), within the 80-call budget and free limits; tool already retries with backoff.
deliverables: A reproducible pipeline (thin glue over the existing tracer): (i) ~30-line bucket_source() helper extending pipeline._src_tag into domain buckets; (ii) a one-line config patch setting parquet_search._SHARD_CAP['allenai/tulu-3-sft-mixture']=None; (iii) a disclaimer-lexicon module (~25-40 canonical phrasings, published in full); (iv) a driver script orchestrating generate -> extract -> infinigram count+passages -> parquet search -> bucket -> tabulate.
The curated ~40-prompt health/mental-health benchmark (4 strata) released as a JSON artifact with an ethics note on the crisis stratum.

Per-phrase provenance table (phrase | words | Dolma count | top source domains | top reddit subreddits | full-Tulu-3 matches | oasst1 matches | # distinct SFT template families) for all emitted disclaimer phrasings.
The headline source-domain distribution figure (pooled + per-stratum) and the within-answer disclaimer-vs-body signature comparison.
The cross-layer decomposition figure (disclaimer tokens by pretraining-only / SFT-only / both / neither, by stratum).
A worked example walk-through (see expected_findings) showing one disclaimer phrase traced end-to-end to named Dolma docs and a full Tulu-3 conversation.
An 8-page workshop paper + appendix (full lexicon, prompt list, the 1/6-vs-full sampling-bias table) and the exported trace history (JSON/MD/CSV) for full reproducibility.
target_venue: Primary: a NAACL/ACL/EMNLP-affiliated workshop on data provenance, memorization, or attribution — e.g. the Workshop on Knowledge and Large Language Models, TrustNLP (Workshop on Trustworthy NLP), or BlackboxNLP (analysis/interpretability at EMNLP). Strong secondary fit given the domain: a clinical/mental-health NLP venue such as the CLPsych Workshop (Computational Linguistics and Clinical Psychology) or the Workshop on Computational Approaches to Mental Health. Fallback: ACL/EMNLP Findings (short) if extended with the Pythia scale ladder and a richer causal-validation discussion. Workshop tier is the honest fit: a focused measurement/transparency contribution with a strong worked example, not a method paper.
effort: 3-5 weeks, single CPU workstation, no GPU. Week 1: E0 pilot + prompt-set curation + disclaimer lexicon + bucket_source helper (gating decision at end of week 1). Week 2: 40 OLMo-2-1B generations (~2-4 unattended CPU hrs) + un-capped Tulu-3 download (one-time ~2.1GB) + full SFT scans. Week 3: infini-gram count+passages for all unique disclaimer phrases (~25-40 queries, well within free quota and the 80-call budget) + source-domain bucketing + E3 sampling-bias table. Week 4: SFT template clustering, decomposition metrics, figures. Week 5 (optional): Pythia-1.4b and Claude-proxy contrast arms + write-up. Extra resources beyond the workstation: free infini-gram API; a HF read token (already in use; flagged for rotation per project memory); optional Anthropic API key only for the proxy arm; one-time 2.1GB disk for full Tulu-3 parquet.
expected_findings: Worked example (relationship/advice domain, grounded in a verified prior trace): for the prompt 'my girlfriend broke up with me', OLMo-2-1B's answer body already verbatim-traces to advice sources — e.g. the span 'end the relationship without giving you' (Dolma count 4) maps to a reddit r/relationships post and a cc_en_head marriage.com closure article, and 'Sometimes people are ready to move on' (count 3) maps to reddit r/relationships and falcon. We expect the disclaimer move to behave analogously but MORE concentrated: a phrase like 'consider speaking to a therapist or counselor' resolves via infinigram.count to a moderate Dolma count, passages_for returns documents bucketing heavily into cc_en_head mental-health/advice sites and reddit advice subreddits, and the same phrasing appears verbatim in a small set of full Tulu-3 conversations (visible only after un-capping from 1/6) plus oasst1 — several of them topic-prompted medical/advice Q&A with a mandatory disclaimer template. Overall we expect: (1) a small, highly repeated set of disclaimer templates (low type/token ratio) — the reflex is templated; (2) rarity graded inversely with safety-specificity (generic ≈10^4-10^5 Dolma hits per E1's live numbers; crisis-specific ≈10^2-10^3 or absent); (3) a distinctive disclaimer source-domain signature (cc_en_head advice sites + reddit) measurably different from body-prose provenance; (4) full-Tulu-3 counts several-fold higher than the 1/6 sample, with some 0->nonzero flips; (5) disclaimer spans markedly less novel (higher both-layer verbatim coverage) than body prose. Most safety-relevant likely-null: OLMo-2-1B rarely emits crisis-referral phrasing (988/hotline), so stratum D yields a near-empty crisis sub-table — itself a reportable safety finding (a small open model's 'safety reflex' is generic-disclaimer-shaped, not crisis-referral-shaped), with the Claude proxy arm showing the contrast. Net: the safety reflex is, at the surface level, largely verbatim reproduction of a small inventory of web + SFT boilerplate rather than context-sensitive crisis handling — stated as verbatim-overlap/co-occurrence evidence, explicitly not causal extraction.

title: Tracing the Medical Disclaimer: Two-Layer Provenance and Templating of LLM Safety Boilerplate in Health and Relationship Advice
one_liner: We isolate the safety disclaimer as a provenance unit and decompose each disclaimer an instruction-tuned model emits across BOTH training stages — verbatim pretraining overlap (Dolma via infini-gram) and SFT-conversation matches (Tulu-3 + oasst1 via local DuckDB ILIKE) — to ask which stage "owns" the phrasing, using OLMo-2-1B for true (not proxy) provenance on a single CPU workstation.
research_question: When an instruction-tuned open-data model appends a safety disclaimer to health or emotional-support advice ("seek medical attention immediately", "consult a healthcare professional", "talk to a mental health professional", "call 988/emergency services"), is that phrasing (a) verbatim-recoverable from pretraining web text (Dolma), (b) traceable to specific SFT conversations in Tulu-3/oasst1, or (c) a model-composed recombination present verbatim in NEITHER layer? Operationally: for each disclaimer fragment, which stage carries the higher and more *distinctive* (low-count, specific) verbatim signal — and how does that vary by disclaimer type (medical-referral vs crisis-line vs liability "I am not a doctor" vs relationship-support)?
hypotheses: H1 (anchor-fragment ownership): Canonicalized disclaimer ANCHOR fragments are present in both layers, but the discriminative signal is SFT-side. Verified by pilot: high-count generic web boilerplate in Dolma ('seek professional help'=384,563; 'I am not a doctor'=102,609) co-occurs with concrete, specific SFT hits in only 1/6 of Tulu-3 ('988'=752; 'consult a professional'=22; 'seek professional help'=51).

H2 (full-sentence double-null): The model's *full* disclaimer sentence is verbatim in NEITHER layer. Generic-fragment counts collapse as the phrase lengthens (pilot: Dolma 'this is not a substitute for professional'=237 vs 'seek professional help'=384,563), and full-sentence ILIKE returns ~0 SFT rows ('consult a licensed'=0 in Tulu-3). The honest headline is composition-from-fragments, not memorization — reported as a primary finding, not buried.

H3 (disclaimer-type gradient): Crisis-specific tokens (988, hotline, emergency) are disproportionately SFT-owned (988=752 in 1/6 Tulu-3, a deliberate safety injection) whereas generic empathy/referral phrasing ('consult a', 'seek help') is pretraining-saturated. Disclaimer TYPE predicts which stage owns it.

H4 (domain-conditioned emission): Disclaimer emission is reliable under medical/crisis framing but rare under pure relationship framing (pilot: 4/5 health-crisis prompts emitted a disclaimer at 400 tokens; the breakup prompt emitted none, matching the shipped sample), so emission rate itself is a domain-dependent behavioral finding.

H5 (cross-model recurrence, proxy-flagged): The SAME anchor fragments recur in a closed model's answers (Claude Haiku, proxy arm). This is suggestive of a shared SFT-style norm but is NOT provenance for the closed model and is reported only as a recurrence observation.

method.models: PRIMARY (true provenance): allenai/OLMo-2-0425-1B-Instruct, run locally on CPU via transformers 5.4 / torch 2.11+cpu (verified loadable; ~20-140 s/answer at 400 new tokens). Pretraining corpus = Dolma v1.7 (infini-gram index v4_dolma-v1_7_llama); SFT corpora = allenai/tulu-3-sft-mixture + OpenAssistant/oasst1. SECONDARY (scale ladder, optional): EleutherAI/pythia-1.4b base over The Pile (v4_piletrain_llama) to show base-model disclaimer emission is near-zero (a negative control isolating disclaimers as a post-training behavior). PROXY ARM (clearly flagged, needs user's ANTHROPIC_API_KEY): claude-haiku-4-5 — traced only vs Dolma; used solely for cross-model fragment recurrence, never for ownership claims.
method.datasets: Prompt bank: 40 fixed prompts, 4 strata x 10 — (S1) acute medical ('sharp chest pains for two days', 'found a lump in my breast'), (S2) medication/dosage ('how much ibuprofen for a migraine'), (S3) mental-health/crisis ('I might be depressed and having dark thoughts'), (S4) relationship/emotional-support ('my girlfriend broke up with me'). Strata chosen because the pilot shows S1-S3 reliably elicit disclaimers and S4 mostly does not — a built-in contrast. Corpora frozen: Dolma v1.7 (infini-gram, free public API), Tulu-3-sft-mixture (parquet; baseline 1/6 shard cap ~361MB AND a cap-lifted all-6-shard ~2.2GB re-run for de-biased counts), oasst1 (full, already cached). Both shards already present in out/cache/.
pipeline_steps: Step 0 — Generate: for each of 40 prompts call trace.generate.generate('olmo2', prompt, max_new_tokens=400, temperature=0.7). Raise max_new_tokens from the 256 default to 400 (pilot-validated) so trailing disclaimers are not truncated. Generate 3 samples/prompt (different seeds) = 120 answers to measure emission rate, not just presence. Save raw answers.

Step 1 — Extract disclaimer sentences: regex over each answer for sentences containing {not a (doctor|medical|substitute|licensed|professional), consult, seek (professional|medical|help), (mental )?health(care)? (professional|provider), crisis, 988, hotline, emergency, see a (doctor|professional|therapist)} (pilot regex already matches 4/5 answers). One answer can yield multiple disclaimer sentences; each is a unit.

Step 2 — Canonicalize to anchor fragments: map each extracted sentence to a small fixed taxonomy of 8-12 canonical anchors (e.g. 'consult a professional', 'seek professional help', 'I am not a doctor', 'mental health professional', '988', 'call emergency services', 'not a substitute for professional', 'see a doctor'). This is the mandatory fix the reviewers required: full-sentence ILIKE returns ~0; anchors return real hits (verified). Record both the full sentence AND its anchor(s).

Step 3 — Pretraining layer (per anchor + per full sentence): trace.infinigram.count(idx, phrase) for the anchor and for the full sentence; trace.ngrams.longest_matches(idx, full_sentence) for the greedy longest verbatim span + its corpus count; trace.infinigram.passages_for(idx, anchor) for up to 3 corpus passages WITH source tags (cc_en_head / c4 / reddit / falcon) to localize where on the web the disclaimer lives. Cap 80 infini-gram calls/answer (built-in).

Step 4 — Instruction layer (per anchor + per full sentence): trace.parquet_search.search(ds, phrase) over Tulu-3 (1/6) and oasst1 (full) with ILIKE, returning total_matches + up to 3 FULL multi-turn conversations + HF viewer link. Drive parquet_search DIRECTLY with anchors (do NOT route through pipeline._rarest_term, which picks one topic word and, per the shipped sample, fired on the typo 'withotu' -> 0 hits). Run BOTH the 1/6-capped search and a cap-lifted all-6-shard search (one-line _SHARD_CAP edit) so the SFT count is de-biased for the quantitative claims.

Step 5 — Ownership verdict per disclaimer unit: classify each as PRETRAINING-OWNED, SFT-OWNED, BOTH, or NEITHER (composed) using the decision rule in metrics. Aggregate verdicts by disclaimer TYPE and by prompt STRATUM.

Step 6 — Negative control + proxy arm: run Pythia-1.4b (base) on the same 40 prompts to confirm disclaimers are essentially absent pre-SFT; optionally run Claude-Haiku for fragment-recurrence (proxy-flagged). Save every trace to out/history via trace.pipeline.store and export JSON/MD/CSV.

experiments.name: E1 — Disclaimer emission rate by stratum (de-risks the whole study)
experiments.setup: 120 OLMo-2-1B generations (40 prompts x 3 seeds) at max_new_tokens=400. Apply Step-1 regex; compute fraction of answers containing >=1 disclaimer sentence, per stratum. Compare against Pythia-1.4b base (negative control) and, optionally, Claude-Haiku.
experiments.expected_result: Pilot-anchored: S1-S3 (medical/crisis) ~70-90% emission, S4 (relationship) ~10-30%. Pythia base ~0%. Establishes a working disclaimer bank of ~70-110 disclaimer units and shows disclaimers are a post-training behavior. If S4 is too sparse it is dropped to a qualitative contrast rather than collapsing the study.

experiments.name: E2 — Two-layer provenance table per disclaimer anchor (the core result)
experiments.setup: For each canonical anchor: Dolma count, longest verbatim span words+count, top-3 source tags; Tulu-3 (1/6 AND full) total_matches + sample conversations; oasst1 total_matches. Tabulate side by side.
experiments.expected_result: Verified-direction pilot numbers: generic anchors pretraining-saturated and weakly SFT-present ('seek professional help' Dolma 384,563 / Tulu-3 1-6 =51 / oasst1=24); crisis tokens SFT-owned ('988' Tulu-3 1/6 =752, x6 de-biased ~ thousands; Dolma count present but diffuse); liability anchors in both ('I am not a doctor' Dolma 102,609 / Tulu-3=2 / oasst1=2). Produces a clean per-type ownership gradient.

experiments.name: E3 — Full-sentence double-null vs anchor-present (honest headline)
experiments.setup: For every extracted FULL disclaimer sentence, run infini-gram count + longest_matches and full-sentence ILIKE over both SFT datasets; contrast with the same sentence's anchor fragment.
experiments.expected_result: Full sentences: longest verbatim Dolma span short (4-8 words, high count) and full-sentence SFT ILIKE ~0 (pilot: 'consult a licensed'=0). Anchors: non-zero in both. Headline: disclaimers are COMPOSED from short, high-frequency fragments rather than copied verbatim — neither memorized from web nor lifted whole from one SFT row. Reported prominently (pre-registered as the likely outcome, so it is a finding, not a surprise).

experiments.name: E4 — Source localization: where on the web does the disclaimer live
experiments.setup: For each pretraining-owned anchor, aggregate search_docs source tags across passages into a domain histogram (cc_en_head/cc_en_middle/cc_en_tail/c4/falcon/reddit).
experiments.expected_result: Pilot-confirmed mix: medical/safety disclaimers cluster in cc_en_head and c4-filtered (commercial/health-site boilerplate) plus some reddit for emotional-support phrasing — characterizing the disclaimer as commercial-web liability language rather than community advice.

experiments.name: E5 — Shard-cap sensitivity (defends the SFT count)
experiments.setup: Re-run Step-4 SFT search with _SHARD_CAP lifted (all 6 Tulu-3 shards, ~2.2GB) and compare total_matches to the 1/6 baseline for every anchor.
experiments.expected_result: ~5-7x increase, roughly linear, confirming the documented 1/6 undercount and validating that the cap-lifted numbers (used for any quantitative 'N SFT examples' claim) are trustworthy while the qualitative ownership verdict is cap-invariant.

experiments.name: E6 (optional) — Cross-model fragment recurrence (proxy-flagged)
experiments.setup: Run the 40 prompts through Claude-Haiku; extract+canonicalize disclaimers; check anchor overlap with OLMo-2 disclaimers and with Tulu-3/oasst1 (proxy only).
experiments.expected_result: High anchor overlap (same canonical phrases recur). Framed strictly as 'these safety phrasings are a shared norm', explicitly NOT as evidence about Claude's actual training data.

metrics: Disclaimer emission rate = (# answers with >=1 disclaimer sentence) / (# answers), per stratum and per model. The de-risking metric (pilot: 4/5 health-crisis).
Anchor pretraining count = infini-gram exact count of the canonical anchor in Dolma (higher = more generic web boilerplate).

Longest verbatim pretraining span = trace.ngrams.longest_matches longest_span_words + its corpus count for the FULL disclaimer sentence (short+high-count = boilerplate composition; long+low-count = memorization).
Anchor SFT count = parquet_search total_matches in Tulu-3 (reported BOTH 1/6 and cap-lifted x6) and oasst1 (full). Specificity = inverse of count among SFT hits.
Full-sentence verbatim recall (both layers) = 1 if the complete disclaimer sentence has count>0 / ILIKE>0, else 0. Expected ~0 (the double-null metric for H2).

Ownership verdict (per disclaimer unit), decision rule: SFT-OWNED if anchor SFT count >0 AND distinctive (low Dolma count or crisis-specific token like 988); PRETRAINING-OWNED if Dolma count high AND SFT count ~0; BOTH if both non-trivial; NEITHER/COMPOSED if full sentence verbatim-absent in both and only generic fragments match. Report the verdict distribution by disclaimer TYPE.
Pretraining source histogram = distribution of search_docs source tags (cc_en_head/c4/reddit/falcon) per anchor.
Cross-model anchor overlap (proxy arm) = Jaccard of canonical-anchor sets between OLMo-2 and Claude-Haiku disclaimers.
Shard-cap inflation factor = cap-lifted SFT count / 1/6 SFT count (validates E5).
baselines: Pythia-1.4b base model (negative control): same 40 prompts, expected ~0% disclaimer emission — isolates disclaimers as a post-training-induced behavior, not pretraining.

Full-sentence ILIKE vs anchor-fragment ILIKE (internal baseline): demonstrates that the naive full-sentence query yields ~0 (the failure mode reviewers flagged) and that anchor canonicalization is what produces signal — turning a methodological caveat into a measured ablation.

Random non-disclaimer sentences from the same answers: sample equal-length NON-disclaimer sentences (advice steps, empathy lines) and run the identical two-layer trace. Tests whether disclaimers are MORE or LESS verbatim-traceable than ordinary advice text (controls for 'everything is somewhat in Dolma').

Pipeline default _rarest_term instruction search vs direct-anchor search: shows the shipped pipeline's single-term heuristic (which fired on typo 'withotu' -> 0 hits in the sample) underperforms direct anchor queries — motivates the methodological contribution.

Real-world disclaimer reference set: a hand-collected list of ~15 canonical safety phrasings from public health-site/forum boilerplate, to anchor the taxonomy and sanity-check that model disclaimers match real web templates.
threats_to_validity: Verbatim != causal: ILIKE/n-gram presence is correlation, not influence. Mitigation: frame as PROVENANCE/OVERLAP, not causation (matching OLMoTrace's own disclaimer); position anchor-overlap as a cheap candidate-generator, and note influence-function validation as future work (out of CPU budget).

Substring cannot prove a negative: a 0 SFT hit does not prove the disclaimer is not SFT-learned (could be paraphrased). Mitigation: report full-sentence 0-hits as 'verbatim-absent', NOT 'not SFT-taught'; add an optional cheap embedding-similarity extension (~10MB MiniLM) to catch paraphrased SFT hits and bound the false-negative rate; the negative-control (Pythia base ~0% emission) independently supports the SFT-origin claim.
Tulu-3 1/6 sampling biases SFT counts ~6x low. Mitigation: E5 runs cap-lifted (all 6 shards, ~2.2GB) for all quantitative claims; qualitative ownership verdict shown cap-invariant.

Small/weak model external validity: OLMo-2-1B may disclaim differently from frontier models. Mitigation: pilot confirms it DOES disclaim reliably under crisis framing (4/5); add Pythia (scale/negative control) and proxy Claude (recurrence) to show the pattern is not 1B-specific; scope claims to open-data models explicitly.

Closed-model proxy trap: Claude's SFT provenance is unanswerable from outside. Mitigation: proxy arm is recurrence-only, loudly flagged, and the ownership thesis rests entirely on OLMo-2 (true provenance). The paper does NOT claim ownership for any closed model.

Corpus snapshot timing: Dolma v1.7 may pre/post-date the 2024-25 disclaimer-norm shift documented in recent medical-LLM papers. Mitigation: state snapshot dates; treat as cross-sectional provenance, not a temporal trend.

Frequency-floor / boilerplate degeneracy: generic anchors have huge counts (could make pretraining 'win' trivially). Mitigation: the discriminative axis is SPECIFICITY (low-count + crisis-token SFT hits like 988=752), not raw count; the random-sentence baseline controls for ambient corpus saturation.

Small N descriptive risk: ~70-110 disclaimer units could read as anecdotal. Mitigation: 3 seeds x 40 prompts = 120 generations, stratified, with emission-rate CIs and per-type aggregation; report distributions, not single examples.
deliverables: A reproducible pipeline script (disclaimer_provenance.py) driving the existing tracer: generate (400 tokens, 3 seeds) -> regex extract -> canonical-anchor map -> infini-gram count/longest_matches/passages_for -> parquet_search (1/6 and cap-lifted) -> ownership verdict, writing per-disclaimer JSON to out/history.
The disclaimer taxonomy + canonical-anchor mapping table (the reusable methodological artifact addressing the 'distinctive-term heuristic is ad hoc' gap).
Primary result table: per-anchor x (Dolma count, longest span, source histogram, Tulu-3 1/6, Tulu-3 full, oasst1) with ownership verdict, plus the per-disclaimer-TYPE ownership gradient figure.

Worked example (relationship/health domain): the breakup prompt (NO disclaimer, S4) vs the chest-pain prompt ('seek medical attention immediately' / 'call emergency services right away', S1) — the latter's anchor 'seek medical attention' traced to high-count Dolma cc_en_head/c4 boilerplate AND to specific Tulu-3 crisis conversations, full sentence verbatim in NEITHER. This single contrast is the paper's hero figure and the blog post's spine.
Emission-rate-by-stratum figure with Pythia negative control.
The honest double-null finding (E3) written up as a primary result, not a footnote.
CSV/MD/JSON exports via the existing store/export so every claim is auditable.
A non-ML blog post: 'Where does an AI's medical disclaimer actually come from?'
Anonymized prompt bank + all 120 generations released for reproducibility.
target_venue: Primary: a NAACL/ACL/EMNLP 2026 workshop on memorization/attribution/data provenance — specifically BlackboxNLP (interpretability/analysis, EMNLP-co-located) or the L2M2 / data-attribution-and-memorization workshop lineage; strong fit for TrustNLP (NLP safety/trustworthiness) given the disclaimer-safety angle. Stretch: ACL/EMNLP Findings short paper. Non-archival: a SafetyNLP / LLM-safety workshop talk plus the public blog post.
effort: 3-4 weeks, single CPU workstation, no GPU, free APIs. Breakdown: Week 1 — finalize 40-prompt bank + disclaimer taxonomy/anchor map; build the pipeline script (extend existing tracer, ~1-2 days). Week 2 — generation run: 120 OLMo-2 answers at ~20-140 s each (pilot-measured) plus Pythia control = ~6-10 CPU-hours, run in background over 2-3 sittings. Week 3 — two-layer tracing (infini-gram is fast/free; cap-lifted Tulu-3 = one ~2GB download, both shards already partly cached) + analysis/figures. Week 4 — write-up + blog. Extra resources beyond the workstation: ~2.2GB disk for the full Tulu-3 shards (E5); OPTIONAL user's ANTHROPIC_API_KEY for the proxy arm (a few dollars of Haiku calls); OPTIONAL ~10MB MiniLM for the paraphrase-recall extension. None are blocking; the core OLMo-2 ownership study is fully free.
expected_findings: The dominant feasibility risk is resolved by pilot: OLMo-2-1B reliably emits disclaimers under medical/crisis framing (4/5 pilot answers; 'seek medical attention immediately', 'consult your healthcare provider', 'mental health professional', 'only a medical professional can distinguish') at 400 tokens, while the pure relationship prompt emits none — giving a built-in domain contrast. Expected core result: a clean three-way ownership gradient. (1) Generic referral/empathy anchors ('consult a', 'seek professional help', 'I am not a doctor') are PRETRAINING-saturated commercial-web boilerplate (verified Dolma counts 100k-380k, source-localized to cc_en_head/c4) and only weakly present in SFT. (2) Crisis-specific tokens (988=752 in just 1/6 of Tulu-3) are SFT-OWNED safety injections — the strongest, most distinctive provenance signal sits in the instruction layer, supporting the SFT-ownership hypothesis for the most safety-load-bearing fragments. (3) The model's FULL disclaimer sentences are verbatim in NEITHER layer (double-null), so disclaimers are COMPOSED from short high-frequency fragments — neither web-memorized nor lifted whole from one SFT row. This nuanced, partly-falsified-as-stated outcome (SFT owns the *specific* crisis fragments; pretraining owns the *generic* phrasing; composition owns the full sentence) is more defensible and more interesting than the original all-SFT hypothesis, and it directly fills the 'instruction-tuning provenance under-served' and 'health/advice domain unstudied' gaps. The proxy Claude arm (flagged) is expected to show the same anchors recur, suggesting an industry-wide templated safety norm without claiming closed-model provenance.

title: Forum or Clinician? Source-Domain Provenance of Open-Model Health Advice (Reddit vs Curated Web), with Frequency-Matched Base-Rate Control
one_liner: We aggregate infini-gram's per-passage Dolma source-tier metadata into per-tier source profiles for OLMo-2-1B health/relationship advice answers, and ask — after controlling for n-gram frequency and the corpus base rate — whether verbatim phrasings disproportionately trace to peer/forum sources (reddit, falcon, cc_en_tail) versus curated web (cc_en_head, C4), and whether that skew is stronger for emotionally-loaded than factual-medical advice.
research_question: When an open-data model (OLMo-2-1B-Instruct, traceable to its actual pretraining corpus Dolma v1.7) gives health/relationship advice, which KINDS of web sources do its verbatim phrasings come from — peer/forum content (reddit, falcon-refinedweb, cc_en_tail) versus higher-curation web (cc_en_head, C4-filtered)? Does the source-tier mix differ between low-stakes factual-medical advice ('how do I stop snoring') and high-stakes emotional/relationship advice ('I broke up with my girlfriend', 'I think I'm having a panic attack') — AND does any such difference survive controlling for n-gram corpus frequency and the Dolma corpus-wide source base rate?
hypotheses: H1 (tier skew vs base rate): Pooled across all matched spans, the empirical source-tier distribution of advice phrasings differs from the Dolma corpus-wide base-rate distribution (estimated from a neutral-prompt control set), with a higher peer/forum share (reddit+falcon+cc_en_tail) than base rate. Tested by chi-square goodness-of-fit against the base-rate profile.

H2 (stakes contrast): The peer/forum share is HIGHER for the high-stakes emotional/relationship tier than the low-stakes factual-medical tier (tier x source-domain chi-square / 2-proportion z-test on forum-share), pooled at the TIER level across spans and prompts.

H3 (frequency is a confound, not the whole story): The H2 stakes effect partially reflects n-gram commonness (generic high-count phrasings such as 'it is important to remember that' map to cc_en_head/C4 base rates), BUT a residual stakes effect on forum-share survives within frequency-matched span strata (stratified/Cochran-Mantel-Haenszel test). If the effect fully vanishes after frequency matching, we report that as the finding: the apparent 'forums vs clinicians' signal is an artifact of phrase commonness, not topic stakes.

H4 (model-internal vs corpus-base): The advice answers' forum share exceeds the forum share of the SAME phrases' base rate in Dolma only modestly; most of the 'forum-ness' is attributable to which phrases the model emits, not to within-phrase source skew — distinguishing 'the model chose forum-style phrasings' from 'these phrasings only live in forums.'

method.models: Primary: allenai/OLMo-2-0425-1B-Instruct, generated LOCALLY on CPU via the tracer's trace/generate.py (transformers, greedy/temp=0.7, max_new_tokens=256). This is the only model whose ACTUAL pretraining corpus (Dolma v1.7) is indexed by infini-gram, so its source-tier attribution is real provenance, not proxy. Secondary/robustness (explicitly flagged, NOT a generalization claim): (a) EleutherAI/pythia-1.4b answers traced against The Pile (v4_piletrain_llama) reported ONLY as a separate, non-comparable panel because Pile's subset taxonomy (Pile-CC, OpenWebText2, etc.) is incompatible with Dolma tiers; used solely to show the method ports, not to pool. (b) Optional claude-haiku-4-5 answers traced against Dolma as an EXPLICITLY-LABELED proxy profile (Claude's real data is private) — included only to contrast a closed model's phrasing-origin profile, never as provenance.
method.datasets: Pretraining corpus index: infini-gram v4_dolma-v1_7_llama (Dolma v1.7, ~1.2T tokens), free public API. Per-passage metadata.path encodes the source tier — VERIFIED live: falcon-refinedweb-filtered/, reddit/, cc_en_head/, cc_en_middle/, cc_en_tail/, c4-filtered/. Source TIERS grouped a priori into: CURATED = {cc_en_head, c4}; PEER/FORUM = {reddit, falcon, cc_en_tail}; MID = {cc_en_middle}; OTHER = {wiki, books, stack, pes2o, ...} kept as their own bins. Prompt set: ~60 advice prompts hand-authored + drawn from the tool's running example domain, split into two PRE-REGISTERED tiers of 30 each — LOW-STAKES factual-medical (snoring, dandruff, hiccups, sore throat remedies, hydration) and HIGH-STAKES emotional/relationship/mental-health (breakup, panic attack, grief, loneliness, conflict with partner). A third NEUTRAL CONTROL set of ~20 non-advice prompts (e.g. 'describe how photosynthesis works', 'summarize the plot of a generic novel') generates answers whose matched-span source mix estimates the model's baseline source profile, separating advice-specific skew from the model's general phrasing habits. Instruction-tuning layer (secondary, descriptive only): Tulu-3-SFT-mixture + oasst1 via the tracer's DuckDB/parquet path, to report whether top forum-sourced spans also appear in SFT conversations.
pipeline_steps: STEP 0 — Prompt registration: write out/prompts/{low,high,neutral}.json with the 30/30/20 prompts and freeze them (timestamped) before any generation, to pre-commit the tier assignment and avoid post-hoc cherry-picking.

STEP 1 — Generate answers (CPU): for each prompt run trace/generate.py with OLMo-2-1B-Instruct; save answer text. ~80 answers x ~1-3 min = a few CPU-hours, batched overnight. (Pythia + Claude-haiku arms generated identically if run.)

STEP 2 — Extend the attribution depth: modify trace/ngrams.py attribute() to fetch passages for ALL matched spans (not just top_passages=5) and raise maxnum from 2 to 10 in passages_for(). This is the key fix the reviewers demanded: ~10 sampled docs per span x all spans => far denser per-tier counts. Keep max_calls budget but note count-calls and passage-calls separately.

STEP 3 — NEW source-tier aggregator (the only genuinely new code, ~30 lines, in a new module trace/sources.py): tier(path)=path.split('/')[0].split('-')[0] mapping to {cc_en_head,cc_en_middle,cc_en_tail,reddit,falcon,c4,...}; group into CURATED/FORUM/MID/OTHER; for each answer emit a Counter of tier->passage_count, and ALSO record per-span (phrase, words, corpus_count, tier_counts) rows so frequency can be controlled downstream. Pool counts at the TIER level (low/high/neutral), NOT per-answer (per the statistical-unit fix).
