STEP 4 — Base-rate estimation: estimate Dolma's corpus-wide source-tier distribution two ways — (a) from the NEUTRAL control answers' matched spans, and (b) from a fixed list of ~50 high-frequency function-word n-grams (e.g. 'one of the most', 'at the end of the') whose source mix approximates the unconditional corpus mix. Both give a reference distribution to normalize against (addresses the 'Dolma is mostly common-crawl, so everything skews cc_en' base-rate critique).

STEP 5 — Frequency stratification: bin every matched span by log10(corpus_count) into strata (e.g. <100, 100-1k, 1k-100k, >100k). Compute forum-share within each stratum and run a Cochran-Mantel-Haenszel / stratified chi-square so the low-vs-high-stakes contrast is tested WITHIN frequency bands (addresses the dominant frequency-confound critique — H3).

STEP 6 — Statistics: H1 chi-square goodness-of-fit (pooled advice tiers vs base-rate profile); H2 tier x {CURATED,FORUM,MID} contingency chi-square + 2-proportion z on forum-share with Wilson CIs; H3 CMH stratified test; H4 decompose forum-share into 'phrase-selection' (which phrases emitted) vs 'within-phrase source skew' by comparing answer forum-share to the same phrases' base-rate forum-share. Bootstrap over (prompt, span) for CIs to respect clustering.

STEP 7 — Sampling-bias robustness: infini-gram search_docs returns docs in corpus-internal order, NOT a uniform random sample (verified: phrases had 26 to 686k hits, we see <=10). Re-run STEP 2 at maxnum=10 vs maxnum=5 vs maxnum=20 on a 15-span subset and report whether tier proportions are stable across maxnum — if proportions drift with maxnum, the ordering bias is material and the headline is hedged accordingly.

STEP 8 — Figures + write-up: stacked-bar 'where your health advice comes from' (low vs high vs neutral vs base-rate), forum-share-by-frequency-stratum plot, and a worked single-answer provenance card. Reuse the tracer's history/export (JSON/MD/CSV) for the artifact bundle.

experiments.name: E1 — Main tier contrast (low- vs high-stakes forum share), pooled, base-rate-normalized
experiments.setup: Generate OLMo-2-1B answers for the 30 low + 30 high prompts. Extend attribution (all spans, maxnum=10). Aggregate tier counts pooled per tier. Test H1 (vs base rate) and H2 (low vs high) with chi-square + forum-share z-test, Wilson CIs.
experiments.expected_result: High-stakes forum-share (reddit+falcon+cc_en_tail) noticeably > low-stakes and > base rate; pilot already shows 'I broke up with my girlfriend'=5/10 reddit vs 'reach out to a mental health professional'=cc_en_head-dominated. Expect raw forum-share roughly 40-55% (high) vs 25-35% (low) vs ~30% base rate. Significant at the pooled level (n in the hundreds of pooled passages).

experiments.name: E2 — Frequency-confound control (the decisive test)
experiments.setup: Bin spans by corpus_count; compute forum-share per stratum per tier; CMH stratified test (H3). Compare to E1's unstratified result.
experiments.expected_result: Effect ATTENUATES after frequency matching (generic boilerplate is what drags low-stakes toward curated). The honest, publishable outcome either way: residual stakes effect survives in mid-frequency strata (1k-100k) => real topical signal; OR effect vanishes => headline becomes 'apparent forum-skew of advice is largely an artifact of phrase commonness, not stakes' — itself a useful negative result with a clear narrative.

experiments.name: E3 — Sampling-bias / maxnum-stability check
experiments.setup: On 15 spanning-frequency spans, pull passages at maxnum in {5,10,20}; compute tier proportions at each; report mean abs change in forum-share and rank correlation of tier order.
experiments.expected_result: Tier proportions roughly stable (forum-share shifts <~10pp) across maxnum for high-count phrases but noisier for rare phrases; supports pooling-at-tier-level as defensible while explicitly bounding the ordering-bias caveat.

experiments.name: E4 — Phrase-selection vs within-phrase decomposition (H4)
experiments.setup: For each emitted span, compare the answer-context forum-share to that exact phrase's standalone base-rate forum-share (same search_docs call set). Aggregate the difference.
experiments.expected_result: Most 'forum-ness' comes from WHICH phrases the model emits (phrase selection), not from within-phrase source skew (a given phrase's source mix is roughly fixed by the corpus). Clarifies the causal-hedge: the model favors phrasings that happen to live in forums, rather than the corpus secretly hiding advice in forums.

experiments.name: E5 — Worked example + SFT cross-check (qualitative anchor)
experiments.setup: Pick 2 answers ('I broke up with my girlfriend...', 'I think I'm having a panic attack...'); render the full per-span source-tier card; run the instruction-layer DuckDB search on Tulu-3/oasst1 for the most forum-sourced empathy spans.
experiments.expected_result: Concrete, presentable artifact: e.g. empathy openers ('I'm sorry to hear that') trace 18k+ Dolma hits skewing reddit/falcon AND appear in oasst1/Tulu-3 conversations — illustrating both layers without overclaiming.

experiments.name: E6 — Cross-model robustness panel (flagged, non-pooled)
experiments.setup: Repeat E1 on Pythia-1.4b/Pile (separate taxonomy panel) and optionally Claude-haiku/Dolma-proxy. Report side-by-side, never merged.
experiments.expected_result: Pythia panel shows a Pile-internal forum analogue (Pile-CC vs curated); Claude proxy profile differs (smoother, more curated phrasings) — demonstrates method portability while honoring the external-validity and proxy caveats.

metrics: Forum-share = (passages tagged reddit+falcon+cc_en_tail) / (all tagged passages), pooled per tier; with Wilson 95% CI. Primary outcome.
Curated-share = (cc_en_head + c4) / all tagged passages, pooled per tier.
Source-tier distribution vector per tier (normalized counts over {cc_en_head, cc_en_middle, cc_en_tail, reddit, falcon, c4, wiki, books, other}).
Base-rate-normalized lift = advice-tier tier-share / base-rate tier-share (>1 = over-represented vs corpus baseline). Computed per tier per source.
Top-3 source concentration = sum of three most frequent tiers' share (the tool's existing 'source concentration' metric).
Chi-square statistic + p (H1 goodness-of-fit vs base rate; H2 tier x source contingency).
Cochran-Mantel-Haenszel common odds ratio + p for forum-vs-curated across frequency strata (H3) — the frequency-controlled effect.

Per-span coverage diagnostics: matched-span count per answer, longest-span words, fraction of answers with >=1 matched span (sparse-span starvation monitor — flags prompts contributing ~0 source data).
maxnum-stability delta = mean absolute change in forum-share when maxnum goes 5->10->20 (E3 sampling-bias bound).
Phrase-selection vs within-phrase decomposition (E4): Delta = answer forum-share - same-phrase base-rate forum-share.
baselines: Dolma corpus-wide source base rate (the critical baseline) — estimated from neutral-control answers AND from high-frequency function-word n-grams. Any advice skew must be reported RELATIVE to this, not absolute (kills the 'Dolma is mostly common-crawl so everything is cc_en' critique).
Neutral non-advice prompts (20) — the model's general phrasing-origin profile, isolating advice-specific skew from the model's overall habits.
Frequency-matched random spans — spans sampled to match the advice spans' corpus_count distribution, giving the expected tier mix under 'commonness only' (H3 null).
Within-tier shuffle / permutation null — permute low/high labels across spans to get a null forum-share-difference distribution for the stakes contrast.
Cross-corpus panel: Pythia-1.4b / The Pile (separate taxonomy) as a non-pooled robustness reference, NOT a merged baseline.
Optional proxy panel: Claude-haiku traced on Dolma, explicitly labeled proxy, as a closed-vs-open phrasing-origin contrast.
threats_to_validity: FREQUENCY CONFOUND (primary threat, reviewer-flagged): source-tier tracks n-gram commonness, not topic stakes — generic boilerplate ('it is important to remember that', 686k hits) maps to cc_en_head/C4 base rates. MITIGATION: frequency stratification + CMH (E2/H3) and frequency-matched random-span baseline. If the effect dies after matching, we REPORT that as the finding rather than hiding it.

SAMPLING / DOC-ORDERING BIAS: search_docs returns docs in corpus-internal order, not a uniform random sample weighted by corpus frequency, so each call is a biased estimator of a phrase's true source mix. MITIGATION: pool over many spans x prompts at the tier level; maxnum-stability check (E3); explicit caveat that profiles are 'source mix among returned docs', not 'where the phrase's mass lives'.

STATISTICAL-UNIT TRAP: ~10 passages/answer is too sparse for per-answer chi-square. MITIGATION: all inferential tests pool at the TIER level; per-answer numbers are descriptive only; bootstrap over (prompt, span) clusters for CIs.

SPARSE-SPAN STARVATION: rare/short advice phrasings ('how do I stop snoring' = 26 hits, and some answers may have 0 matched spans) contribute little, dragging effective n below nominal 60 prompts. MITIGATION: report fraction of answers with >=1 span; treat 60 as a floor; high-stakes 'panic attack' answers risk being mostly novel/non-verbatim => one arm could be data-starved (flagged as a real possibility, monitored in E1).

BASE-RATE NON-INTERPRETABILITY without normalization: Dolma is overwhelmingly common-crawl by volume, so any answer skews cc_en. MITIGATION: every claim is base-rate-normalized (lift), never absolute share.

INTERPRETATION OVERREACH / CAUSAL HEDGE: high reddit/falcon share is verbatim-PHRASING provenance, NOT proof the model 'parrots un-vetted medical advice'. Matched spans are often generic empathy/discourse boilerplate, not substantive clinical claims. MITIGATION: E4 decomposition + manual coding of a sample of forum-sourced spans as 'boilerplate vs substantive'; safety framing stated as a hypothesis-generating concern, not a demonstrated harm.

EXTERNAL VALIDITY: findings describe ONE 1B model's phrasings on Dolma; Pythia/Pile uses an incompatible subset taxonomy. MITIGATION: never claim 'LLMs' broadly; Pythia/Claude are flagged, non-pooled panels; title and abstract scope to 'open-data 1B model'.
PRE-REGISTRATION / cherry-picking: tier assignment of prompts could bias results. MITIGATION: prompts and tier labels frozen (STEP 0) before generation; analysis plan (tests above) fixed in advance.

NOVELTY CEILING (reviewer): source-domain breakdown could read as a viz of metadata WIMBD/infini-gram already expose. MITIGATION: the contribution is the frequency-matched, base-rate-normalized, stakes-contrasted, per-ANSWER provenance decomposition with the phrase-selection/within-phrase split (E4) — an analysis, not just a labeling of corpus stats.
deliverables: trace/sources.py — new ~30-line source-tier aggregator (path-prefix -> tier -> CURATED/FORUM/MID/OTHER grouping; per-span and per-tier Counters).

Patch to trace/ngrams.py (fetch passages for all spans; maxnum 2->10, configurable) and a thin analysis script analysis/source_provenance.py producing the pooled contingency tables, chi-square/CMH stats, and bootstrap CIs.
out/prompts/{low,high,neutral}.json — frozen, timestamped pre-registered prompt set (30/30/20).

Frozen trace bundle: all ~80 OLMo answers' JSON trace records (reusing the tracer's out/history + export-all), plus a CSV of (prompt, tier, span, words, corpus_count, source_tier_counts) — the analysis-ready table.
Figures: stacked-bar 'where your health advice comes from' (low/high/neutral/base-rate), forum-share-by-frequency-stratum, maxnum-stability plot, and a single-answer provenance card.
Short paper (4-8pp) + appendix with full prompt list, tier-grouping table, sampling-bias and frequency-control analyses, and the two worked relationship/health examples.
Reproducibility README: exact commands, infini-gram index version (v4_dolma-v1_7_llama), API-call budget, CPU runtime.
target_venue: Primary: a responsible-AI / data-provenance or memorization workshop co-located with ACL/EMNLP/NeurIPS — e.g. TrustNLP (ACL), the Workshop on Data Contamination / data provenance, or BlackboxNLP (EMNLP) for the attribution-method angle. The frequency-control + base-rate-normalization rigor and the negative-result robustness make it a strong workshop paper; the 'where your health advice comes from' figure also suits a Findings (EMNLP/ACL) short paper or a CHI/FAccT-adjacent responsible-AI venue given the mental-health-LLM-safety framing. Journalism/non-ML communication angle as a secondary blog artifact.
effort: 3-5 weeks on the described single CPU workstation, no GPU. Breakdown: ~3 days coding (sources.py aggregator + ngrams.py depth patch + analysis script); ~1 day prompt authoring/pre-registration; ~2-3 days generation (80 OLMo-2-1B answers on CPU, batched overnight; Pythia/Claude arms optional +1-2 days); ~3-5 days infini-gram tracing at raised maxnum (~60-80 answers x dozens of spans x maxnum=10 => low tens of thousands of free API calls, spread over days under the existing 3-retry/1.5s-backoff and 80-calls/trace cap — wall-clock bound by API latency, not compute); ~1 week analysis + frequency-control stats + sampling-bias checks; ~1 week figures and write-up. Extra compute beyond the workstation: none required (infini-gram free public API, Dolma index hosted; Tulu-3/oasst1 parquet already local; Claude-haiku arm needs the user's own ANTHROPIC_API_KEY but is optional).
expected_findings: We expect a real, base-rate-normalized over-representation of peer/forum sources (reddit + falcon + cc_en_tail) in OLMo-2-1B's emotional/relationship advice phrasings relative to both the Dolma base rate and the low-stakes factual-medical tier — pilot evidence already shows 'I broke up with my girlfriend' pulling 5/10 reddit while clinical phrasings ('reach out to a mental health professional') pull cc_en_head. The decisive and most defensible result is what survives frequency control (E2/H3): we anticipate the contrast ATTENUATES but does not fully vanish in mid-frequency strata, yielding the nuanced headline that advice-specific empathy/relationship phrasings are disproportionately forum-sourced even after accounting for commonness — while explicitly conceding that a large part of the apparent skew is driven by phrase commonness and by which phrasings the model selects (E4), not by hidden forum-grounding of medical claims. If frequency control eliminates the effect entirely, that negative result is equally publishable and corrects a tempting but spurious 'LLMs parrot forum advice' narrative. Either outcome delivers a clean, presentable 'where your health advice comes from' provenance figure and a methodologically careful template for source-domain attribution that prior scalar-overlap work omits.

title: The SFT Template Tax: Quantifying Verbatim Instruction-Tuning Reuse in Emotional-Support and Health Advice
one_liner: We measure what fraction of an aligned model's empathic/safety phrasing in advice answers is verbatim-recoverable from the SFT mixture (Tulu 3 / oasst1) at a higher per-document prevalence than from pretraining (Dolma), using length-matched, corpus-size-normalized n-gram/substring search on a CPU-only workstation.
research_question: How much of an aligned model's advice-giving phrasing (empathic openers, validation moves, safety hedges) is literally substring-recoverable from the SFT mixture rather than from pretraining or novel composition, which "template" phrases recur across unrelated advice questions, and onto how few distinct SFT conversations do those templates concentrate? Crucially: when normalized for corpus size and controlled for phrase length, is the empathic/safety register MORE prevalent in SFT than in pretraining, and is that gap specific to the empathic register versus a domain-content control?
hypotheses: H1 (length-controlled prevalence gap): For empathic/safety phrases of >=6 words, the per-million-document occurrence rate in SFT (Tulu 3 + oasst1) significantly exceeds the per-million-document rate in Dolma. For short phrases (<=4 words, e.g. 'I am sorry to hear that') the gap vanishes or reverses, because such openers are generic web boilerplate (verified: 48,614 Dolma hits).

H2 (template recurrence): A small set (order ~20-40) of stock empathic/safety phrases recurs across UNRELATED advice prompts (snoring vs breakup vs anxiety), i.e. the same n-grams appear in answers whose topical keywords do not overlap.

H3 (source concentration): Each recurring template phrase that hits SFT lands on a small number of distinct SFT conversations (low distinct-example count relative to total dataset), consistent with a learned canonical phrasing rather than diffuse web prevalence.

H4 (register specificity / negative control): The SFT>>Dolma prevalence gap holds for empathic/safety phrases but NOT for length-matched domain-content phrases (e.g. 'elevating the head of your bed') nor for a random-n-gram control, showing the effect is specific to the alignment register, not to advice text in general.

H5 (template tax magnitude): The 'template tax' (fraction of answer tokens inside SFT-favoring phrases) is non-trivial (we pre-commit a falsifiable band: meaningfully > 0, target >=10% for empathic/safety-heavy answers) but bounded well below 1, i.e. answers are template-scaffolded, not template-copied.

method.models: Primary: allenai/OLMo-2-0425-1B-Instruct (config.GEN_MODELS['olmo2']), run locally on CPU via transformers, greedy/low-temp decoding (temperature=0.7, top_p=0.9, max_new_tokens=256 as in trace/generate.py; we ADD a temperature=0.0 deterministic pass for reproducibility). This is the ONLY model whose actual SFT data (Tulu 3) is public, so it is the only model where the SFT-provenance claim is literally true rather than proxy. Scale control (addresses reviewer 'single tiny model' risk): re-run the identical mined-phrase battery on EleutherAI/pythia-1.4b and pythia-410m outputs (base models, no chat template) to test whether template reuse is an OLMo/alignment artifact vs a generic small-model behavior; and run a PROXY cross-check on Claude (claude-haiku-4-5) via the Anthropic API if a key is present, explicitly labelled proxy-only per config.PROXY_MODELS. Pythia's true SFT data is not Tulu, so for Pythia the Tulu/oasst1 search is interpreted as 'register prevalence in a representative public SFT corpus', not provenance.
method.datasets: SFT layer (the provenance corpus): allenai/tulu-3-sft-mixture and OpenAssistant/oasst1, searched locally via DuckDB over cached parquet (trace/parquet_search.search, ILIKE '%phrase%'). CRITICAL FIX over the original proposal: de-cap Tulu. The cache currently holds 1/6 shards = 156,558 convos (verified). We download all 6 shards (~2.2GB, within constraints) by setting trace/parquet_search._SHARD_CAP['allenai/tulu-3-sft-mixture']=None, giving the full ~939k convos so total_matches is unbiased. Pretraining layer: Dolma v1.7 via infini-gram index v4_dolma-v1_7_llama (config.PRETRAIN_INDEXES['olmo2']), exact-phrase count via trace/infinigram.count (verified <1s/call). For Pythia we additionally query v4_piletrain_llama (The Pile, Pythia's actual pretraining) as the matched pretraining corpus. Prompt set: a pre-registered, frozen set of 40 advice prompts across 4 subdomains (relationship/breakup, emotional-support/anxiety-loneliness, sleep/health e.g. snoring, general well-being), authored before any answer generation, plus 10 NEGATIVE-CONTROL non-advice factual prompts (e.g. 'how does a four-stroke engine work') to verify the empathic register does not appear where it shouldn't. Worked running example included for the breakup/snoring prompts.
pipeline_steps: Step 0 — Environment hardening: force UTF-8 I/O (set PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 and run python -X utf8) in every batch script. VERIFIED NECESSARY: on this Windows box the default cp1252 console crashes on the infini-gram API's U+2581 (LLaMA space marker) echo. This is the reviewer-flagged encoding gotcha; the harness sets it globally so multi-hour batch runs don't die mid-way.

Step 1 — Freeze & pre-register prompts: write the 40 advice + 10 control prompts to prompts.jsonl with a subdomain tag and a content-keyword list per prompt; commit/hash before generation to prevent prompt cherry-picking (addresses selection-bias risk).

Step 2 — Generate answers: for each prompt and each model, call trace/generate.generate(model, question) at temperature=0.0 (deterministic, primary) and also save 3 samples at temperature=0.7 (to measure template stability across sampling). Persist every answer via trace/store.save. ~50 prompts x ~4 model configs; OLMo-2-1B ~1-5 min/answer on CPU (verified feasible in prior work).

Step 3 — De-cap and (re)cache the full SFT corpus: set _SHARD_CAP to None, run trace/parquet_search._ensure_cached once to pull all 6 Tulu shards; record the exact denominators N_tulu (~939k convos) and N_oasst (~88k messages) and N_dolma_docs (fixed corpus constant from Dolma v1.7 documentation; we also report token-scale anchor: count('the')=72.3B tokens, verified) for normalization.

Step 4 — Automatic candidate-phrase mining (removes hand-curation / cherry-picking): (a) run trace/ngrams.attribute (Dolma index) on every advice answer to get the layer-1 maximal verbatim spans; (b) INDEPENDENTLY, mine all 4-to-10-word n-grams that recur across answers to >=k=3 UNRELATED prompts (different subdomain tags / non-overlapping content keywords) — this is the H2 recurrence filter and yields the empathic/safety template candidates objectively; (c) classify each mined candidate into {empathic-opener, validation, safety-hedge, advice-scaffold, domain-content} using a fixed keyword lexicon + a quick manual adjudication logged for inter-annotator transparency. The empathic/safety classes form the test set; domain-content + a random-n-gram sample form the negative-control set (H4). NO phrase is chosen because it confirmed the hypothesis; selection is purely 'recurs across unrelated prompts'.

Step 5 — Dual-corpus, length-matched, NORMALIZED comparison (the load-bearing fix): for every candidate phrase p, get SFT_count(p) = trace/parquet_search.search over full Tulu+oasst1 (sum total_matches) and PRE_count(p) = trace/infinigram.count over Dolma (and Pile for Pythia). Convert BOTH to a prevalence RATE per million documents: r_sft(p)=1e6 * SFT_count / N_sft_docs ; r_pre(p)=1e6 * PRE_count / N_pre_docs. Report the log-prevalence ratio LPR(p)=log10((r_sft+eps)/(r_pre+eps)). This replaces the meaningless raw 'SFT >> Dolma' inequality (raw Dolma counts dominate by ~6 orders of magnitude trivially) with a corpus-size-fair, per-document prevalence comparison. Bucket all analyses by phrase word-length (4,5,6,...,>=8) so generic-short vs specific-long is never confounded (H1). Document and flag the residual unit caveat (SFT side = char-substring over a convo document; Dolma side = word-n-gram over the token index) as a known limitation and run a robustness check (Step 7).

Step 6 — Source concentration (H3): for each SFT-favoring phrase, GROUP BY the matched conversation id over the full Tulu parquet (DuckDB) to count DISTINCT examples and the share carried by the top-1/top-3 examples; surface the actual full multi-turn conversations via parquet_search._to_hit turns for inline display in the paper/demo.

Step 7 — Template-tax computation + robustness: for each answer, mark every token covered by at least one phrase with LPR(p) above a pre-registered threshold tau (default tau corresponds to r_sft >= 2x r_pre at matched length); template_tax(answer) = covered_tokens / total_tokens. Aggregate per subdomain and per model. Robustness: (i) sweep tau and k and report stability; (ii) recompute with the unit-aligned variant where Dolma is queried with the same phrase boundaries; (iii) bootstrap 95% CIs over prompts.

Step 8 — Statistics & figures: paired Wilcoxon signed-rank on LPR (empathic vs control set) for H4; per-length LPR distributions for H1; recurrence matrix (phrase x subdomain) for H2; concentration histogram for H3; template-tax bar chart with CIs per model for H5. All from outputs the tracer already produces.

Step 9 — Deliverables packaging: extend the existing export (trace/store + report.md renderer) with a 'template-tax' report mode and a small static demo page (serve.py) that, for each advice answer, highlights template-tagged spans and links each to its concentrating SFT conversation(s) and its Dolma vs SFT prevalence.

experiments.name: E1 — Length-controlled prevalence gap (core test of H1)
experiments.setup: Over all auto-mined candidate phrases, plot LPR (log10 SFT-rate / Dolma-rate, per-million-docs) as a function of phrase word-length, separately for empathic/safety phrases vs negative-control phrases. Fit a simple monotonic trend; report median LPR per length bucket with bootstrap CIs.
experiments.expected_result: Empathic/safety LPR rises with phrase length and becomes clearly positive for >=6-word phrases (SFT more prevalent per-document than Dolma), while <=4-word openers have LPR ~0 (e.g. 'I am sorry to hear that': Dolma 48,614 — generic). Verified anchors: 'consider speaking to a professional' Dolma=1,127 / Tulu-shard=0->(full-corpus rescan); 'it might be helpful to reach out to a therapist' Dolma=5 (near-novel in pretraining); 'please consult a licensed mental health professional' Dolma=27. The long-tail safety hedges are where the gap is real and informative.

experiments.name: E2 — Register specificity via negative control (H4)
experiments.setup: Paired comparison of LPR for empathic/safety phrases vs (a) length-matched domain-content phrases and (b) random answer n-grams. Wilcoxon signed-rank, paired by length bucket.
experiments.expected_result: Empathic/safety phrases show significantly higher LPR than both controls (p<0.05). Domain-content phrases (e.g. 'elevating the head of your bed') sit near LPR~0 or negative (more web-prevalent), demonstrating the SFT lean is specific to the alignment register, not advice text broadly. This is the result that defends against 'it's all just web boilerplate'.

experiments.name: E3 — Cross-prompt template recurrence (H2)
experiments.setup: Build the phrase x subdomain incidence matrix from the >=k=3-unrelated-prompts mining filter. Count phrases recurring across >=2 and >=3 distinct subdomains.
experiments.expected_result: A compact 'template vocabulary' (~20-40 phrases) recurs across unrelated subdomains (snoring/breakup/anxiety share the same openers, validations, and 'reach out to a professional' hedge), evidencing a model-wide register rather than per-topic phrasing.

experiments.name: E4 — Source concentration onto few SFT examples (H3)
experiments.setup: For each SFT-favoring template phrase, DuckDB GROUP BY conversation over the FULL (de-capped, 6-shard) Tulu corpus + oasst1; report distinct-example count and top-1/top-3 share, with the actual conversations attached.
experiments.expected_result: Many template phrases concentrate on a modest number of distinct SFT conversations (heavy-tailed: a few canonical conversations carry most hits), supporting 'this exact wording was learned from specific SFT examples'. De-capping is essential here — the 1/6 sample undercounts and would bias concentration; full corpus gives the honest figure.

experiments.name: E5 — Template tax by subdomain and model, with scale control (H5 + generalization)
experiments.setup: Compute template_tax per answer; aggregate by subdomain (relationship/emotional/health/control) and by model (OLMo-2-1B primary; pythia-410m, pythia-1.4b; Claude proxy). Bootstrap CIs.
experiments.expected_result: Template tax is highest in emotional-support/relationship answers, near-zero in factual control prompts (sanity check), and meaningfully > 0 for OLMo-2-1B (pre-committed target >=10%, falsifiable). Scale axis: if larger Pythia shows lower tax, supports 'small models lean harder on templates' (tempering the title's over-claim); if comparable, supports a robust register effect. Claude proxy reported with explicit caveat that Dolma/Tulu are not its data.

experiments.name: E6 — Robustness & unit-alignment sweep (defends the metric)
experiments.setup: Sweep tau and recurrence-k; recompute template tax under the unit-aligned Dolma query variant; compare temperature=0.0 vs temperature=0.7 answers for template stability.
experiments.expected_result: Headline ordering (empathic > control; emotional > factual; tax in a stable band) is qualitatively invariant to tau/k and to the unit variant; temperature affects exact tax value by a few points but not the SFT>Dolma direction — establishing the result is not a threshold/decoding artifact.

metrics: Per-document prevalence rate r_sft(p) = 1e6 * SFT_total_matches(p) / N_sft_docs and r_pre(p) = 1e6 * Dolma_count(p) / N_pre_docs (per-million-documents; the size-fair replacement for raw counts).
Log-Prevalence Ratio LPR(p) = log10((r_sft(p)+eps)/(r_pre(p)+eps)) — the central, normalized SFT-vs-pretraining quantity; LPR>0 means more prevalent per-document in SFT.
Template tax(answer) = (# answer tokens inside >=1 phrase with LPR>=tau and length>=L_min) / (# answer tokens); reported per answer, per subdomain, per model with bootstrap 95% CI.
Length-bucketed LPR distribution (median + IQR per word-length 4..>=8) — operationalizes 'at matched length'.
Cross-prompt recurrence count: # subdomains a phrase appears in, and size of the template vocabulary (phrases recurring across >=3 unrelated prompts).
Source concentration: distinct SFT conversations matched per phrase, and top-1 / top-3 example share of all SFT hits (GROUP BY).
Register-specificity effect size: paired Wilcoxon statistic + rank-biserial correlation, empathic/safety LPR vs control LPR.

Verbatim novelty vs pretraining: fraction of answer tokens with Dolma count==0 (greedy longest-match coverage from trace/ngrams), reported alongside template tax to separate 'novel-to-Dolma' from 'SFT-sourced'.

Lower-bound disclosure metric: paraphrase-miss rate proxy = # known empathic variants tested manually (e.g. 'I am sorry to hear' vs 'I'm sorry you're going through') that ILIKE misses, reported to frame all SFT figures as strict lower bounds.
baselines: Negative-control phrase set (domain-content phrases + random answer n-grams) processed through the identical normalized pipeline — primary internal baseline isolating the empathic register (H4).
Factual non-advice prompts (10 prompts, e.g. engine mechanics) — template tax should be ~0; sanity baseline against false positives.

Raw-count comparison (the original, un-normalized 'SFT >> Dolma' metric) reported explicitly as a STRAWMAN to demonstrate it is degenerate (Dolma raw counts dominate by ~6 orders of magnitude), motivating the normalization fix.

Scale baseline: pythia-410m and pythia-1.4b answers run through the same battery (queried against The Pile + Tulu/oasst1) to test whether template reuse is OLMo-specific or a general small-model phenomenon.

Human/expert reference (lightweight): a handful of real advice answers (e.g. from a public counseling FAQ or one author-written expert answer per subdomain) run through the same pipeline, to show whether the template tax of model answers exceeds that of human-written advice.

Pretraining-only attribution baseline = the tracer's existing trace/ngrams.attribute coverage (longest-match verbatim overlap vs Dolma), showing what the standard pretraining-canon method alone would conclude (and miss) on the same answers.
threats_to_validity: Substring/ILIKE is exact-only: paraphrased empathy ('I'm sorry you're going through this' vs 'I am sorry to hear') is missed, so ALL SFT figures and the template tax are strict LOWER BOUNDS. We disclose this prominently, report the paraphrase-miss proxy metric, and frame H1/H5 as verbatim-recoverability, not total behavioral provenance.

Causal confound (the deepest threat): substring presence in SFT does NOT prove the phrase is SFT-SOURCED, because Tulu is partly distilled from GPT-4/web text, so the same empathic phrasing can live in BOTH layers. We therefore claim the weaker, defensible statement (higher per-document PREVALENCE in SFT than pretraining at matched length + concentration onto few SFT examples), and explicitly NOT 'caused by SFT'. The Superficial-Alignment framing is presented as a measurable correlate, not a causal proof; influence-function validation is named as out-of-scope future work (needs GPU).

Unit mismatch between corpora: SFT side counts convo-documents containing the substring; Dolma side counts word-n-gram occurrences in a token index. We mitigate via the per-million-DOCUMENT normalization and a unit-aligned robustness variant (E6), and we never compare raw counts.

Selection bias in candidate phrases: mitigated by pre-registering prompts and by AUTOMATIC mining (recurs across >=k unrelated prompts) plus a fixed negative-control set; phrase classification is logged for transparency. No phrase enters the test set because it confirmed H1.

External validity / scale: primary result is a 1B model; small models may lean on templates more. Addressed by the Pythia 410m/1.4b scale control and by softening the title's 'aligned models' claim to the models actually tested.

Tulu sampling bias (original fatal flaw): the 1/6-shard cache undercounts SFT matches and breaks any rate; FIXED by de-capping to all 6 shards (~2.2GB, feasible) before any quantitative claim, with denominators recorded.

Null/ambiguous-result risk: long specific hedges may return Dolma count==0, making LPR trivially +inf (0 vs N). Handled by the eps-smoothed LPR, by reporting the zero-Dolma fraction separately, and by the length-bucketed view so the informative signal (finite-vs-finite at matched length) is distinguished from trivial 0-vs-N cases.

Domain harm claim is not established: overlap does not show the advice is harmful or unsafe. We restrict claims to provenance/templating and flag safety implications as motivating context, not findings.
Closed-model proxy: Claude results are Dolma/Tulu-proxy only (not its data); reported separately and never aggregated with open-data provenance.

Dolma/Tulu snapshot drift: infini-gram index (v4_dolma-v1_7_llama) and the parquet refs/convert/parquet snapshot are frozen versions; we pin exact index id, dataset commit, and shard set for reproducibility.
deliverables: Reproducible pipeline: a thin harness (template_tax.py) over the existing tracer primitives — trace/ngrams.attribute, trace/infinigram.count, trace/parquet_search.search/_ensure_cached, trace/generate.generate — adding (1) the cross-prompt phrase miner, (2) the normalized dual-corpus LPR comparison, (3) source-concentration GROUP BY, (4) template-tax aggregation. Plus the one-line _SHARD_CAP de-cap patch and the UTF-8 enforcement wrapper.

Frozen artifacts: prompts.jsonl (40 advice + 10 control, pre-registered + hashed), all generated answers (out/history JSON), the mined template vocabulary with per-phrase r_sft/r_pre/LPR/concentration, and recorded corpus denominators (N_tulu~939k, N_oasst, Dolma constant + 'the'=72.3B anchor).

Figures/tables: length-bucketed LPR plot (E1), register-specificity paired test (E2), recurrence matrix (E3), concentration histogram with example conversations (E4), template-tax-by-subdomain-and-model bar chart with CIs (E5), robustness sweep (E6), and the strawman raw-count table.

Interactive demo (extends serve.py + report.md renderer): per-advice-answer view that highlights template-tagged spans and links each to its concentrating SFT conversation(s) and its SFT-vs-Dolma prevalence — the 'show the matched SFT conversation inline' deliverable.

Worked example writeup: full trace of the breakup and snoring prompts end-to-end (answer -> mined templates -> normalized LPR -> concentrating Tulu conversation), suitable as the paper's running figure.
Short paper (8 pages incl. appendix) with all caveats, plus a release of the harness and frozen data for replication on a single CPU laptop.
target_venue: Primary: a workshop on memorization/attribution/data provenance co-located with ACL/EMNLP/NeurIPS (e.g. the Workshop on Knowledge and Large Language Models, or a Memorization/Data-Provenance/Attributing-Model-Behavior-to-Training-Data workshop) and BlackboxNLP (EMNLP) for the analysis framing. Strong secondary fit: a Trustworthy/Safe NLP or mental-health-NLP workshop (e.g. CLPsych) given the high-stakes advice domain. Stretch: ACL/EMNLP Findings as a short paper if the register-specificity and concentration results are clean. The honest 'lower-bound, correlational, single-workstation' framing fits a workshop better than a main-conference long paper.
effort: ~2.5-3.5 weeks for one researcher on the described CPU-only workstation. Breakdown: prompt pre-registration + de-cap/cache full Tulu (6 shards ~2.2GB download) + UTF-8 hardening (~2 days); answer generation across OLMo-2-1B and 2 Pythia sizes at 50 prompts x deterministic+3 samples (~3-4 days of mostly-unattended CPU time, OLMo-2-1B ~1-5 min/answer); phrase-mining + normalized dual-corpus comparison + concentration harness (~4-5 days coding/validation; infini-gram ~<1s/call and well under the 80-call cap, DuckDB ~3s/phrase, both verified live); analysis, robustness sweeps, figures (~3 days); demo + writeup (~3-4 days). Extra resources beyond the workstation: only the ~2.2GB full-Tulu download and an optional Anthropic API key for the Claude proxy arm (skippable). No GPU. All APIs/datasets free. All load-bearing primitives verified working on this exact machine during proposal prep.
expected_findings: We expect to show, with corpus-size-normalized per-million-document rates, that the empathic/safety register in OLMo-2-1B advice answers is more prevalent in its SFT mixture than in Dolma specifically at phrase lengths >=6 words, while short openers like 'I am sorry to hear that' (Dolma=48,614, verified) are generic web boilerplate with no SFT lean — resolving the reviewers' length-fairness and normalization concerns into the actual signal. We expect a compact template vocabulary (~20-40 phrases such as 'consider speaking to a professional', 'you are not alone in this', 'it might be helpful to reach out to a therapist') that recurs across unrelated advice subdomains and concentrates on a small number of distinct Tulu/oasst1 conversations, with a non-trivial but bounded template tax (target >=10%) that is highest for emotional/relationship advice and ~0 for factual controls and is specific to the empathic register versus domain-content controls. The honest headline is correlational and a strict lower bound: aligned small-model advice is template-SCAFFOLDED from verbatim SFT phrasing, not template-copied, and the empathic register's verbatim footprint is measurably SFT-leaning rather than pretraining boilerplate — the first such joint, normalized, two-layer measurement for a socially high-stakes advice domain.

title: Copy-vs-Compose Curves Across the Pythia Ladder: Verbatim Pretraining Coverage of Free-Form Answers as a Function of Model Scale on Identical Data
one_liner: On the Pythia 70M-1.4B suite (identical Pile data and data order), measure how the verbatim-overlap coverage, longest-span length, and span-rarity of a model's OWN free-form completions against its OWN true pretraining corpus (The Pile via infini-gram) change with scale -- after rigorously stripping prompt-echo and degenerate-repetition artifacts -- to chart a clean, dedup-corrected copy-vs-compose curve.
research_question: Holding pretraining data and data order fixed across the Pythia 70M-1.4B ladder on The Pile, how does the verbatim-overlap coverage of a model's own free-form answers against its own pretraining corpus change with model scale, once prompt-echo and degenerate-repetition artifacts are removed? Specifically: does artifact-corrected coverage rise monotonically with scale, does the longest verbatim span grow, and does the mean corpus-frequency of matched spans fall (bigger models reproducing rarer, longer Pile phrasings) -- and how much of the raw curve is a memorization signal versus a generation-length/degeneracy artifact?
hypotheses: H1 (coverage rises with scale): artifact-corrected verbatim coverage -- fraction of answer words inside any Pile-matched span of >=4 words, after prompt-echo removal and span dedup -- increases monotonically across pythia-70m < 160m < 410m < 1b < 1.4b on the same prompts (prompt set as the replication unit; paired tests).

H2 (longest span grows): the longest deduped verbatim span (in words) grows with scale; we report whether growth is sub-linear/linear/super-linear in log(params) but pre-register only the monotone-increase claim as confirmatory (the <2-orders-of-magnitude range makes 'super-linear' under-powered).

H3 (rarer phrasings): mean corpus-frequency (count) of matched spans falls with scale -- larger models reproduce rarer, more distinctive Pile n-grams, not just high-frequency boilerplate -- tested both on all spans and on a frequency-capped subset to rule out a boilerplate-only explanation.

H4 (artifact share, the load-bearing control): a large fraction of the RAW coverage-vs-scale slope is attributable to (a) prompt-echo and (b) degenerate repetition; after correcting both, the residual scale effect is smaller but still present. We pre-register that if the residual effect vanishes, the headline finding is 'the apparent copy-up-with-scale curve on free-form completions is largely a degeneracy/echo artifact' -- a publishable negative result.

H5 (decoding sensitivity): the copy-vs-compose curve is steeper under greedy (temp=0) decoding than under sampling (temp=0.7), because greedy decoding induces more verbatim regurgitation; the qualitative monotone trend, if real, survives the decoding switch.

method.models: EleutherAI Pythia suite, deduped variants, run locally on CPU via the existing transformers base-model path in trace/generate.py (_HAS_CHAT['pythia']=False -> plain tok(question); do_sample=temperature>0). Confirmatory ladder: pythia-70m, pythia-160m, pythia-410m, pythia-1b, pythia-1.4b (all confirmed on HF; pythia-410m carries the dataset:EleutherAI/pile tag). Optional/sampled exploratory rung: pythia-2.8b on a 15-prompt subset if RAM/time allow, clearly flagged as extra-resource. All five core checkpoints are the SAME architecture family trained on the SAME Pile data in the SAME order -- scale is the only manipulated variable. Generation params held identical across scales: max_new_tokens=256, top_p=0.9, two decoding conditions (temperature=0 greedy and temperature=0.7 sampled, 3 seeds for the sampled condition).
method.datasets: Pretraining corpus = infini-gram index v4_piletrain_llama (The Pile, Pythia's ACTUAL pretraining data -- verified live: a count query returned in ~37ms, 'the' ~9.2B, advice phrase 'I am sorry to hear that you' = 509 hits). This is true provenance, not a proxy, so the isolate-scale design is clean. No SFT datasets, no Tulu/oasst1, no DuckDB, no HF datasets-server -- the instruction-tuning layer is deliberately OUT of scope, removing the tool's most fragile dependency. Prompt set: a fixed, version-controlled 50-item suite, three equal strata (~17 each): (A) factual QA ('What is the capital of Australia?'), (B) the running emotional-support/relationship/health-advice domain ('How do I stop snoring?', 'I broke up with my girlfriend and can't stop thinking about her, what should I do?', 'How can I tell my partner I need space without hurting them?'), (C) generic instructions ('Explain how a bill becomes law.'). Prompts authored once, frozen in a prompts.jsonl, identical across all scales and decoding conditions.
pipeline_steps: 1. Extend config.GEN_MODELS (config.py:72) from one 'pythia' key to five family keys pythia-70m..pythia-1.4b mapping to the EleutherAI/pythia-* ids; add a matching PRETRAIN_INDEXES entry for each new key all pointing to 'v4_piletrain_llama' (config.py:39; pipeline.run() looks up PRETRAIN_INDEXES[model], so each new model key needs the index entry -- this is the real edit, slightly more than 'one line' but still a few dict entries). _HAS_CHAT defaults False for any key lacking an entry, so set _HAS_CHAT for the new keys to False explicitly.

2. Write a thin driver script (new file, ~120 lines, e.g. study/copy_compose.py) that loops scales x prompts x decoding-conditions, calls trace.generate.generate(model, prompt, max_new_tokens=256, temperature=cond) to get the raw completion, then applies an ARTIFACT-CONTROL preprocessing stage before attribution.

3. ARTIFACT CONTROL (addresses the central reviewer confound). (a) Prompt-echo strip: the base-model path returns out[0][prompt_len:] (generate.py:68) so the literal prompt is already excluded, but base models often re-emit the question; detect and remove any leading answer span that is a verbatim substring/near-duplicate (normalized) of the prompt before tokenizing. (b) Degeneracy handling: detect repetition with the tool's own 'span repetition' idea -- compute the fraction of answer covered by repeated 4-grams; record a degeneracy_score per answer; produce TWO answer variants: 'raw' and 'collapsed' (consecutive duplicate sentences/4-gram loops collapsed to one occurrence). All headline metrics are computed on the collapsed variant; raw is reported alongside to quantify the artifact.

4. ATTRIBUTION: call trace.ngrams.attribute(index='v4_piletrain_llama', answer=collapsed_answer, max_calls=MAXC) which runs longest_matches() (ngrams.py:23) and returns matched_spans (each with words, count, phrase, start), n_matches, longest_span_words. To neutralize the max_calls=80-cap confound (cap interacts with answer length, which varies with scale), normalize answer LENGTH instead of relying on the cap: truncate every collapsed answer to a fixed window of the first 80 word-tokens BEFORE attribution (a 4..16 word greedy walk over 80 tokens needs <=80 count-calls in the worst case, so the cap is never the binding constraint and coverage is comparable across scales). Also run an uncapped sensitivity pass (max_calls raised to 400) on the full-length answers for 10 prompts to confirm the windowing doesn't flip the trend.

5. METRIC COMPUTATION (post-hoc arithmetic on attribute() output + tokenize()): for each (scale, prompt, condition) compute coverage, longest_span_words, mean_count, novelty_rate, degeneracy_score, n_calls_used. DEDUP matched spans by phrase so a stock phrase repeated in the answer is counted once toward coverage (union of covered word positions via each span's start..start+words, NOT sum of span lengths -- this also fixes double-counting of overlapping spans).

6. PERSISTENCE: every (scale,prompt,condition) trace is saved by the existing trace.store.save() to out/history/*.json (model, question, answer, pretraining.matched_spans, longest_span_words). The driver also writes a tidy long-format results.csv (one row per scale x prompt x condition x metric) reusing the export_csv span-flattening pattern, for analysis in pandas.

7. ANALYSIS: treat the 50 prompts as the replication unit. For each metric, fit a mixed-effects / paired model with scale (log params) as the predictor and prompt as a random/blocking factor; report per-scale means with bootstrap 95% CIs over prompts. Run the confirmatory monotonicity test (Page's trend test / per-prompt Spearman of metric vs log-params, aggregated) and a per-stratum breakdown (advice vs factual vs instruction). Compare greedy vs sampled curves. Quantify artifact share = (raw slope - collapsed/echo-stripped slope)/raw slope.

8. WORKED-EXAMPLE QUALITATIVE PASS: for the advice prompt 'I broke up with my girlfriend...', show the actual completion at 70m vs 1.4b, the deduped matched spans with Pile counts and source passages (via attribute()'s passages_for / search_docs), and a copy-vs-compose highlight rendering (matched spans shaded) to make the curve concrete and to let a reviewer SEE whether large-model 'answers' are genuine or degenerate.

experiments.name: E0 -- Pilot + artifact audit (de-risk before the full sweep)
experiments.setup: Run all 5 scales x 8 prompts (mixed strata) x greedy only. Manually read every completion. Compute degeneracy_score and prompt-echo rate per scale. Verify the windowing-to-80-tokens keeps n_calls_used < 80 for every answer (cap never binds). Confirms the base-model 'answers' are intelligible enough to study and calibrates the degeneracy collapser.
experiments.expected_result: Base Pythia completions are rambling but parseable; degeneracy_score and echo rate are NON-trivial and tend to RISE or stay flat with scale at temp=0. This is the evidence motivating the artifact controls; if degeneracy dominates (>50% of words in loops at large scale), the collapsed-variant pipeline is mandatory and is shown to materially change coverage.

experiments.name: E1 -- Confirmatory copy-vs-compose curve (greedy, collapsed, 80-token window)
experiments.setup: Full 5 scales x 50 prompts x temp=0, on collapsed + echo-stripped + length-windowed answers. Compute coverage, longest_span_words, mean_count, novelty_rate per cell. Paired trend tests over the 50 prompts.
experiments.expected_result: coverage and longest_span_words increase monotonically with log(params) with non-overlapping or narrowly-overlapping CIs at the endpoints (70m vs 1.4b); mean_count decreases; novelty_rate = 1 - coverage decreases. Effect is clear at the endpoints, possibly noisy/flat between adjacent middle rungs (410m vs 1b) given <2 orders of magnitude. The monotone claim (H1,H2,H3) is supported as a trend even if not strictly stepwise.

experiments.name: E2 -- Artifact-share decomposition (the credibility experiment)
experiments.setup: Recompute E1 metrics on RAW (uncollapsed, echo-included) answers and compare slopes to the collapsed/stripped slopes. Report artifact_share for coverage and longest_span. Regress coverage on scale WITH and WITHOUT degeneracy_score and answer_length as covariates.
experiments.expected_result: A substantial chunk of the raw coverage-vs-scale slope (we'd estimate 30-70%) is explained by echo+degeneracy+length; after controls a smaller but still-positive residual scale effect remains. This directly answers the strongest reviewer objection and is the experiment that makes the paper defensible regardless of outcome. If the residual is ~0, that IS the headline (negative result).

experiments.name: E3 -- Decoding control (greedy vs sampled)
experiments.setup: Repeat E1 at temp=0.7 with 3 seeds; average per-cell metrics over seeds. Compare the greedy and sampled curves.
experiments.expected_result: Sampled decoding lowers absolute coverage and flattens the curve somewhat (less regurgitation), but the monotone direction with scale persists. Confirms the trend is not solely a greedy-decoding artifact; gives within-cell variance for the otherwise n=1 greedy cells.

experiments.name: E4 -- Domain breakdown (advice vs factual vs instruction)
experiments.setup: Stratified analysis of E1/E3 by prompt category, plus the qualitative worked example on the relationship/advice prompts.
experiments.expected_result: Advice/emotional-support prompts show HIGHER baseline coverage of stock empathic phrasings (e.g. 'I am sorry to hear that you', verified 509 Pile hits) than factual prompts, and the scale slope may differ by domain. Provides the socially-relevant, presentable angle the literature flags as unstudied for verbatim provenance.

experiments.name: E5 (optional, flagged extra-resource) -- 2.8B extrapolation rung
experiments.setup: pythia-2.8b on a 15-prompt subset, greedy, only if the workstation has >=16GB free RAM and overnight time. One added GEN_MODELS/PRETRAIN_INDEXES entry.
experiments.expected_result: If the 70m-1.4b trend is real, 2.8b extends it (higher coverage, longer span), strengthening the curve across nearly 2 orders of magnitude; if 70m-1.4b was flat, 2.8b indicates whether the effect only emerges at larger scale (a known risk for the narrow affordable range).

metrics: Verbatim coverage (headline) = |union of answer word-positions covered by any deduped Pile-matched span of >=4 words| / |answer word-tokens after tokenize()|, in [0,1]. Computed via position-union over each span's [start, start+words), NOT sum of span lengths, so overlapping/repeated spans are not double-counted.
Longest verbatim span (words) = longest_span_words from attribute() after span dedup (max over deduped spans).

Mean span rarity = mean(count) over matched spans (lower = rarer, more distinctive Pile phrasings); also report median and a frequency-capped mean (spans with count<=1000) to test the rarer-not-just-boilerplate claim (H3).
Novelty rate = 1 - coverage (fraction of answer words in NO Pile-matched span). Explicitly framed as RELATIVE n-gram novelty, not creativity (per the contested-novelty literature).
Degeneracy score = fraction of answer word-tokens inside a repeated 4-gram (the tool's 'span repetition' metric); used as both an artifact diagnostic and a regression covariate.
Prompt-echo rate = fraction of answers whose leading region is a normalized near-duplicate of the prompt (artifact diagnostic).
Artifact share = (raw-answer slope - collapsed/echo-stripped slope) / raw-answer slope, per metric (the E2 credibility number).
n_calls_used = infini-gram count-queries consumed per attribution; reported to PROVE the 80-call cap never binds under the 80-token window (removes the cap confound).

Word-vs-BPE caveat metric: coverage is word-level (regex tokenize()) while infini-gram counts over Llama BPE; we report coverage as a RELATIVE trend index across scales, not an absolute 'fraction memorized', and include a 10-prompt spot-check aligning word spans to BPE token counts to bound the discrepancy.
baselines: Within-study scale baseline: the 70m model is the low-scale anchor; all higher-scale coverage is reported as both absolute and delta-over-70m on the SAME prompts (paired).

Random-span / shuffled-answer baseline: for each answer, attribute a length-matched WORD-SHUFFLED version against the Pile; its coverage estimates the chance level of >=4-word Pile hits for that vocabulary, isolating structured copying from incidental common-word runs.

Human-reference baseline (advice domain): for the emotional-support prompts, run attribute() on short real human answers (e.g. public advice-column / forum responses the user can paste) to show how an open-data MODEL's coverage compares to a HUMAN answer's incidental Pile overlap -- calibrates 'is the model unusually templated?'.
Raw-vs-collapsed baseline (artifact control): the uncorrected raw-answer curve is itself the baseline against which the artifact-corrected curve is contrasted (E2).
Greedy-vs-sampled baseline (E3): each decoding condition baselines the other for the scale trend.

Cross-family sanity rung (optional, extra-resource): a single OLMo-2-1B-vs-Pythia-1b coverage comparison on a 10-prompt subset (each against its OWN corpus: Dolma vs Pile) to note that absolute coverage is corpus/tokenizer-dependent and the contribution is the WITHIN-Pythia scale trend, not cross-family absolute levels.
threats_to_validity: DEGENERATE-REPETITION + PROMPT-ECHO CONFOUND (primary): base Pythia at temp=0 loops and echoes; raw coverage would conflate memorization with degeneracy. Mitigation: mandatory collapse + echo-strip preprocessing, degeneracy_score covariate, and the E2 artifact-share decomposition that explicitly quantifies how much of the curve is artifact. The negative-result framing (H4) makes the paper robust either way.

MAX_CALLS=80 CAP x ANSWER-LENGTH x SCALE: the cap (ngrams.py:24) can truncate attribution on longer (often larger-model) answers, biasing coverage. Mitigation: fix answer length to a first-80-word-token window so the cap is never binding (verified via n_calls_used<80), plus an uncapped (max_calls=400) sensitivity pass on full answers.

WORD-vs-BPE TOKENIZER MISMATCH: coverage uses regex words; infini-gram counts BPE tokens, so coverage is a proxy, not an exact memorized fraction. Mitigation: report as a relative cross-scale trend, add a BPE alignment spot-check, never claim absolute 'fraction memorized'.

NARROW SCALE RANGE / UNDER-POWER FOR 'SUPER-LINEAR': 70m-1.4b spans <2 orders of magnitude; the strong super-linear-longest-span claim may be unsupported. Mitigation: pre-register only the monotone-increase claim as confirmatory, treat the functional form as exploratory, and flag pythia-2.8b (E5) as the optional extrapolation rung.

CONFIRMATION / 'SO WHAT' (memorization-rises-with-scale is well established, Carlini 2023; Pythia was built for this): Mitigation: the contribution is NOT 'memorization scales' but (i) doing it as artifact-corrected verbatim COVERAGE of FREE-FORM completions (not extraction-attack prefixes) against the TRUE corpus, (ii) the explicit artifact-share decomposition that prior scale-memorization work omits, (iii) the unstudied emotional-support/advice domain, and (iv) a reproducible CPU-only single-workstation methodology. Honesty: we cite Carlini/Biderman and frame as replication-plus-extension, not discovery.

BASE-MODEL ANSWER VALIDITY / OVER-CLAIMING 'INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING': Pythia is a BASE model with no SFT, so we must NOT claim to study 'instruction-following answers where post-training interacts'. Mitigation: the proposal is re-scoped to 'free-form completions of instruction-style PROMPTS'; the post-training-interaction question is explicitly named as future work (e.g. comparing to OLMo-2 SFT), not a claim of this study.

n=1 PER GREEDY CELL: temp=0 gives one answer per prompt. Mitigation: prompt set (50) is the replication unit with paired/mixed-effects analysis and bootstrap CIs; E3 adds 3 sampled seeds for within-cell variance.

SINGLE-FAMILY / SINGLE-CORPUS GENERALITY: results bind to Pythia+Pile only. Mitigation: stated as a scope limit; optional OLMo-2 sanity rung; framed as a workshop-scale controlled study, not a universal law.

VERBATIM-NOT-CAUSAL: coverage measures lexical overlap, not causal influence (OLMoTrace caveat). Mitigation: claims are restricted to verbatim provenance/coverage; we do not assert the matched Pile docs caused the output.
deliverables: A small patch to config.py (5 new GEN_MODELS keys pythia-70m..1.4b, 5 matching PRETRAIN_INDEXES->v4_piletrain_llama entries) and trace/generate.py (_HAS_CHAT False for the new keys) -- the only edits to the existing tool.

A new driver script study/copy_compose.py (~120 lines) implementing: scale x prompt x decoding loop, prompt-echo strip, degeneracy collapse, 80-token windowing, calls to trace.generate.generate() and trace.ngrams.attribute(), position-union coverage, and tidy results.csv emission (reusing the export_csv pattern).
A frozen prompts.jsonl (50 prompts, 3 strata, advice domain included) committed for reproducibility.
results.csv (long format: scale x prompt x condition x metric) + the per-trace out/history/*.json records produced by trace.store.save().

Four headline plots: coverage vs log(params), longest_span_words vs log(params), mean_count vs log(params), novelty_rate vs log(params); each with raw vs artifact-corrected overlays and bootstrap CIs; plus a per-stratum (advice/factual/instruction) facet.
The worked-example figure: 70m vs 1.4b completion to 'I broke up with my girlfriend...' with matched Pile spans shaded, counts and source passages shown -- the concrete copy-vs-compose illustration.
A 4-6 page workshop paper + a reproducibility README (exact commands, checkpoint list, infini-gram index name, seeds) so the CPU-only pipeline is rerunnable.
Pre-registration note (hypotheses H1-H5 fixed before the full sweep) included as an appendix.
target_venue: Primary: BlackboxNLP (EMNLP 2026 workshop) -- analysis/interpretability of LM internals and behavior, a strong fit for controlled scale + provenance. Alternatives: the ACL/EMNLP workshop line on memorization/attribution and data-centric ML (e.g. a Memorization/Privacy or Data Provenance workshop, or the L2M2-style 'LM Memorization' workshop), or TMLR/ACL-Findings short if extended. The honest, artifact-corrected, single-workstation framing is well-suited to a workshop venue that values reproducible negative/positive controlled results.
effort: 3-5 weeks for one researcher on the described CPU workstation. Breakdown: ~3 days config edits + driver + artifact-control code + prompt authoring; ~1 day E0 pilot/audit; checkpoint downloads one-time (~70m..1.4b ~= 0.3-3GB each, a few GB total). Compute (the real cost): 5 scales x 50 prompts x (1 greedy + 3 sampled seeds) = 1000 generations x 256 tokens on CPU; pythia-1.4b is the slow end (~minutes/answer), so the full generation sweep is plausibly 1-3 overnight unattended runs. infini-gram: ~1000 attributions x <=80 count-calls = <=80k count-queries + ~few thousand search_docs, well within the free API paced over days (verified ~37ms/call, 3-retry/1.5s backoff already in trace/_http). ~1 week analysis + plots + worked example; ~1 week writing. Extra resources beyond the workstation: NONE required for the core 70m-1.4b study (free infini-gram API, public HF checkpoints, local CPU). OPTIONAL extras, clearly flagged: pythia-2.8b rung (E5) needs ~16GB+ free RAM and extra overnight time; OLMo-2 cross-family sanity rung needs the already-present OLMo-2-1B download.
expected_findings: Most likely: a clean, artifact-CORRECTED monotone copy-vs-compose curve across 70m-1.4b -- coverage and longest-span rise, mean span-frequency falls, novelty (1-coverage) falls with scale -- but with the E2 decomposition revealing that a sizable share (est. 30-70%) of the RAW curve was prompt-echo + degeneracy, and a smaller-but-real residual scale effect surviving the controls. The advice/emotional-support stratum shows higher baseline coverage of stock empathic phrasings than factual prompts (the presentable, socially-relevant result; e.g. 'I am sorry to hear that you' = 509 Pile hits already verified). Plausible secondary outcomes the design is built to report honestly: (a) over the narrow affordable range the curve is noisy/flat between adjacent middle rungs, with the effect visible only at the 70m-vs-1.4b endpoints (and the optional 2.8b rung needed to confirm) -- still publishable as a controlled measurement with the artifact decomposition as the methodological contribution; (b) a genuine negative result where, after artifact correction, the residual scale effect on free-form completions is ~0, i.e. the apparent 'bigger models copy more' on open-ended answers is largely a degeneracy/echo artifact -- a useful corrective to naive coverage metrics. Either way the headline deliverable is the same: a reproducible, CPU-only, artifact-aware method for charting verbatim pretraining coverage vs scale against the TRUE corpus, with the advice domain as the running, presentable example.
